There were great beasts who stood too tall to see their faces, whose footsteps shook the ground as they passed by, and who could knock down a mighty tree just by their mere passing. The Father knew that the people of this world would need greater order to survive against these monstrosities and helped them organize to fight the monsters, but it was not enough. The Mother taught them greater creativity to make more powerful weapons and different ways of fighting in order to combat the terrible creatures, but it was not enough.

The Parents discussed the peril that these monsters presented to Alacar, and that's when they came to a decision: they would have a child. The Father insisted that this child would need to be more powerful than the monsters, able to match their terrible strength, their impressive speed, and their near invincible resilience. The Mother insisted that this child would need to have abilities and powers the monsters couldn't hope to combat, such as magic, superior intelligence, an insatiable hunger, and the ability to breathe pure energy from their maw. Thus Talir, God of the Dragons and The First, was born of the Parents.

Talir flew through the air, spewing clouds of poison, jets of flame, bolts of lightning, gouts of acid, shards of ice, and more still at the colossal creatures. They hunted the terrible monsters relentlessly, and with each of their enormous foes they defeated, they fed. When they had their fill, they laid a clutch of eggs, from which were born the first dragons. Talir continued the cycle across the land, fighting, killing, and feeding. They continued to sup on the flesh of these powerful creatures, and they continued to lay eggs. Eventually, they filled the world with all manner of dragons, with each clutch reflecting the nature of the creature Talir consumed before they laid those eggs. These first dragons shared their parents hunger, as reflected in their new nature. Thus, dragons became greedy, their need to consume and collect driving them through their long lives.

At first, the people were grateful for any reprieve from the enormous creatures that had been oppressing them, hailing Talir as a literal godsend. But nothing lasts forever, and as the massive monsters became fewer and farther between, soon the people of Alacar forgot the days of yore and instead became as frightened of Talir and their dragons as they had once been of the monsters that hunted them. In the throes of their fear, the people turned their anxiety into violence. And so they started to fight the dragons born of Talir, thinking these new creatures to be nothing more than some kind of new horror. The Parents realized their mistake, and they sided with the mortals, banishing their firstborn and preventing them from ever coming near Alacar again. Talir flew circles around the world, looking for a way in but finding none.

Though the Parents' actions served to keep Talir at bay, their many dragons remained across the world. Some slumbered for centuries, waiting out the anger of mortals in hope of a safer and more peaceful world, while others fought against the mortals who turned against them, harassing the mortals and causing them great strife. The Parents understood far too late that they had given their child too much power. If the mortals were going to find harmony, they needed powerful forces to guide them, but forces that would counter each other and keep each other in check.

Thus the Parents created the Balance, a system for the world whereby the world could become relatively peaceful but ever-changing. They wished for a world where mortals could find fulfillment through hard work and self-improvement. Rather than have one powerful, willful child, they instead gave birth to several groups of children, all of whom would balance each other.

The first Children born to the Balance were Aoz and Zoa, the twins, to whom the Parents gave dominion over life and death. Through their ministrations, Aoz and Zoa were able to balance the carnage on Alacar and allow for a more natural flow of vital energy. Working together, they created a balance in nature and for mortals, creating a cycle of life and death in each part of the world. Thus we give birth, die, and find harmony with the world around us, allowing all creatures a chance to survive, live their lives, and then pass on when their time is up.

Soon after the birth of Aoz and Zoa, Talir came up with an unbelievable yet somehow plausible solution for a being as powerful as Talir to once again re-enter Alacar. The plan was as simple as it was outrageous: they would fly around Alacar so fast that they could reverse time and go back to the world before The Parents arrived, then wait for their own progenitors in ambush. They planned to consume The Parents, freeing themselves from their control and spawning their most powerful clutch of dragons ever. Unfortunately for the scheming First, the Mother realized what Talir was trying to do and told the Father, who came up with a plan to thwart Talir's schemes.

In order to stop Talir from going back in time, the Parents birthed the Three Siblings of Time, known as the Three Sisters of Time in this age: Herst, Awanna, and Dremira. The Three Sisters keep time flowing, keep Alacar's future on track, and prevent unwanted time travelers such as Talir from going back in time to change the past. The eldest of the three sisters is Herst, the master of the past; she keeps an account of everything that has ever come to pass, records that information, and safeguards these secrets within her domain. Awanna is the middle sister and master of the present; she helps mortals to live in the moment as well as to experience this wonderful world around them. The youngest sister is Dremira, master of the future; she helps mortals look forward and dream of what could be. The three of them knew what they needed to do as soon as they were born. Herst prevented Talir from reaching the past, while Awanna slowed down the dragon deity and forced them to live only in the present. Dremira warned Talir of what their future held if they continued to defy The Parents and upset the Balance.

But the dragon deity wouldn't listen to Dremira's warnings. They weren't ready to give up yet. Talir was infuriated by this interference, and so they vowed to devour The Parents. But The Parents knew their child too well. And so they birthed even more children who could help imprison Talir for good: Baoyan, Than Dat, Mului, Thongfa, and Kuu. These five represented five energies that corresponded roughly to at least eight of the twenty primordial elements of existence, as understood before the Balance: fire, earth, and wood; water, air, and electricity; and darkness and space, respectively.

Baoyan acted first, bathing Talir in a raging firestorm, weakening and distracting them from what was to come. Then, Than Dat encased Talir in layers upon layers of solid rock. Once the rock had hardened, Mului covered the rock with jagged ice that would slash Talir and chill them to the bone if they ever escaped their stone prison. Thinking to the future, Thongfa gave this prison air so that powerful sentinels could live on the surface of it and patrol it endlessly. Finally, Kuu steadied the prison in the void of space, just outside of Alacar but yet apart from it, so that Talir would forever be cursed to stay near the world they yearned for, the world of their birth, but never part of it again. Thus the blue moon of Talinos came to orbit Alacar, out of reach of mortals and dragons alike, but a reminder of how far The Parents will go to bring stability to Alacar, even at the expense of their own Child.

The five Siblings then descended to Alacar to rest and maintain their power in the world. Baoyan still roams the world, causing wildfires and erupting volcanoes. Than Dat travels where she is needed, helping the plants grow and keeping the soil healthy for farmers. Mului settled in the deep parts of the ocean, jealously guarding his domain. Thongfa flies around Alacar from moment to moment, painting the world with wind, storms, and weather. And Kuu... Kuu is rarely seen. They tend to dwell in ruins from ancient civilizations, lingering in the stillness of forgotten places and becoming lost in self-reflection.

Finally, the world knew peace. People carried on their day-to-day activities: farming, gathering, building, living, and dying. But the Mother grew bored; she wanted excitement and change for the world. She approached the Father and asked him for the world to constantly grow and develop so that things wouldn't be stagnant for mortals. They could experience little changes from month to month over the course of each year. Father insisted that the cycle repeat so that mortals would know the changes were reliable and could plan around them.

Thus the seasons were born, and alongside the seasons were four new children to monitor and maintain them: Kasa, Chira, Chakisa, and Wera. Kasa freezes the world in Winter, causing animals to flee in migration or hide and hibernate. He challenges the resilience and resourcefulness of mortals in order to survive his harsh conditions. Next comes Chira, who brings new life each Spring. She gives mortals relief and the promise of new life, which makes spring the perfect time for us to go planting. Chakisa is the lazy traveler who brings the heat and light of Summer. It's because of Chakisa that you want to sleep through the middle of the day, cool in the shade. And last is Wera, the guardian of Autumn. She teaches us that through hard work and preparation, we can survive the coming Winter. Others will tell you that the seasons existed before the Balance came to Alacar, but you should not believe them, for I speak the truth: they were born only with these new children. Of course, all of this happened many, many years ago.

Since then, the Mother and the Father have seen that we mortals needed more guidance and more gods to help serve the Balance and bring both change and stability to our lives. We all have heard of Hubitza, the Bloody General, who brings war to mortals as they fight over land, gold, and power. But don't be afraid, little ones. While we have peace, we can bask in the tranquility of Semelleh, who brings calm and contentment.

But be careful not to let that contentment make you lazy, little ones. For decades, we enjoyed the bounties of Ebrugeses, Goddess of Feasts. She lavished us with bountiful crops, game, and gold. She ensured we would want for nothing. But we started to neglect our duties; when the storms came, they destroyed our storehouses, bringing hunger and strife that winter. Kasa opened the door for Havath, as they say, and we barely survived until spring. Since then, we've been mindful to keep Havath away, for he brings famine and starvation. Those in turn lead to desperation, which calls Hubitza to our doorstep, and we don't want that, no. Now we honor Ebrugeses each harvest and on the first day of the new year. We hold a feast in her name but are careful not to overindulge.

And you, little ones, are familiar with Amon, Master Reveler, are you not? I know you'd like to play and have fun all day, and that's the voice of Amon whispering in your ear. He encourages us to have fun, relax, and enjoy all there is to enjoy about living. But his brother, Castilli, is important too; you can't get anything done if you're playing all day. Who will clear the crab traps? Who will pick the coconut off the ground before it spoils? Who will help gather tall grass to repair the roof? Castilli, Master of Restraint, helps remind us of our duties and responsibilities. I know you think he's boring and mean, but you need him to take care of you and keep you focused on doing your chores. It is this Balance that lets us live our lives: hard work and relaxation are both important.

So here we are with a plethora of deities to take care of us, challenge us, and make our lives interesting. There are some who still believe in all sorts of other faiths. But these are not the ways of our family, little ones. Give thanks to Father for his grand designs. Give thanks to Mother for upsetting those designs and keeping our lives exciting.

Now run along and bring back plenty of junu berries for tomorrow's breakfast.

Myth of the Balance: Truth or Fiction?

Although many accounts of the Balance resemble Elder Dyna's in broad strokes while diverging only in the specifics, not everyone in Alacar subscribes to these prevalent beliefs. Some believe Elder Dyna's story is just that—a story.

Indeed, there are many who believe that the claims promulgated by the Balance's clergy (as well as historians, influenced by Herst's ability to warp and control mortal understanding of the past) are nothing more than self-serving lies at best and malicious propaganda at worst. Alternate theories propose differing prehistories from what Elder Dyna suggests. For instance, some theorize that the colossal ancient creatures the Father and Mother purported to have battled never existed at all. While there are bones, shells, and other physical artifacts of their passing beyond any possible tampering by Herst, these theorists suggest that those objects could have easily been fakes, constructed by beings with the power and talent of the deities of the Balance.

Others accept the existence of the enormous creatures as fact but suggest that these ancient enemies were in fact created by the Father and Mother in order to make people scared and dependent on them, allowing them to solve the problem they fabricated by birthing Talir and then, when Talir inevitably presented more dangers, to solve that problem by creating a tyrannical oppressive system, the Balance, that the weary people of the world were far more willing to accept than they would have been if these interdimensional invaders suddenly appeared and forced the Balance on everyone without preamble or machinations.

These theories aren't especially prevalent, even in religions like the Eld that predate the Balance, and many clergy of the Balance dissuade others from promulgating them, suggesting that Enigmas or other malevolent entities are the ones focused on spreading these "malicious lies."

For their part, Balance theologians suggest that if the Parents were going to fabricate such a thing, they wouldn't have made themselves look as sloppy and callous to their own Child as the stories suggest they were in their treatment of Talir. This level of family squabbling, they argue, is too true to life to be a simple falsehood. Skeptics retort that this is exactly what makes the lie so compelling.

Popular Gods In The Isles

The Balance is the predominant religion in The Isles, though some still worship the Eld, enigmas, other deities, or just aspects of the world itself. A fisher may choose to worship the sea itself, and religious scholars might argue whether they are worshiping Mului, the element of water from the Eld, or some other aquatic deity. But to the fisher, the name doesn't matter, and it's just the sea.

Religion is important to many in the Indigo Isles, and its presence is marked in every aspect of people's lives. In smaller communities, the meeting hall is also a temple to The Parents, with the Father instituting a system of customs and traditions while the Mother looks to change the community for the better. The artisans of the Isles ask the Father for guidance and the Mother for inspiration. After all, a boat needs to be waterproof and sturdy, but the images and stories carved on the hull give the boat its artistry.

Aoz and Zoa, the twins of Life and Death, are ever-important on the Indigo Isles. For residents, the circle of life and death is important in all aspects of daily life. The fish live to eat the kelp and keep the harbors clear for ships to pass, while the ships catch the fish to feed the people. The residents then bury the fish bones with newly planted seeds, helping the seedlings grow faster and become trees. They then cut down those trees and turn them into boats to catch more fish in turn.

Far-thinking residents of the Isles can see this cycle and realize that everything they do is in harmony with the circle of life and death. Thus, they respect Zoa and death, even though they miss their loved ones who have passed. They can see their parents' faces in the smiles of their children, and with that, they are content until the cycle turns again and carries them into Zoa's waiting hands.

Amon and Castilli have helped to give balance to the daily lives of residents of the Indigo Isles. While living there takes hard work, they toil during working hours before taking a break to enjoy the sun, surf, and fruit of their land or to throw festivals and parties. Residents also celebrate plenty of holidays and festivals, especially in the city of Rumplank. While Castilli is mostly invoked as a warning to lazy children, Amon's presence is always felt, as the residents know how to enjoy the life they've been given. Leisure time is considered extremely important to mental health and well-being and should not be interrupted by work except in times of dire need.

Likewise, Ebrugeses is often worshiped for the feasts and plenty that she brings to residents of the Indigo Isles, but only occasionally. She is usually only invoked to bless a feast during one of the many holidays and festivals. Havath, lord of famine, rarely shows his face in the Indigo Isles. Sometimes a major storm can ruin crops or scare the fish away, and blights have been known to make for tough years, but the residents are resourceful. They often trade and help neighboring settlements survive in tough times, weakening Havath's power over them.

The climate of the Indigo Isles is relatively stable year-round, as the islands don't experience as drastic of seasons as other parts of the world, though rainfall does vary significantly throughout the year. As such, residents of the Indigo Isles sometimes worship Chakisa as a patron of the Isles rather than specifically as a summer deity, as he is worshiped in other lands. Thus, a significant number of homes contain a small shrine, icon, or symbol of Chakisa, but his worship is less important than that of other deities as the stable climate is almost a given.

Some deities vary from settlement to settlement, generally based on the settlement's major industries and pastimes. Those whose primary focus is on fishing, ocean travel, and trade often worship Mului. They ask the terrifying ocean god for safe passage and to partake in some of his fish. In return, they give offerings of offal and song, praising the ocean god for his might. They sing sea shanties to the ocean, hoping to placate the mighty waves and asking for calm waters or bountiful fish. Families particularly devoted to Mului bury their dead at sea so that their spirits may swim with The Hungry Deep and their bodies may nourish fish and crustaceans.

Sailors and farmers alike ask for boons from Thongfa, hoping to catch her interest and earn her aid. They ask her to run along their ships, bringing a strong wind for sailing, and they thank her as her tears rain down on their crops. Shrines to Thongfa can be found around farms and carved into the wood around the masts of ships. Settlements in the mountains sometimes build tall monuments to the Stormbringer, in hopes that her capricious nature doesn't turn to ire, bringing wind storms and lightning and causing massive destruction. If they're lucky, she wouldn't destroy her own monument.

Than Dat also earns reverence from farmers, as she keeps their crops healthy and helps the trees grow tall. Shrines to Treesinger can always be found around areas of farmland, but they're also quite common in the workshops of shipwrights and woodcarvers. After all, not only does Than Dat help them grow fruit to eat, she also provides shade from the harsh sun and plants to build homes and boats. Families with a tradition of agriculture revere the Earth Mother above all others and bury their dead in the ground so that they may return to her and bring fortune to their progeny by fortifying the soil.

Baoyan keeps the Indigo Isles warm on chilly nights, but he also sends rivers of fire through the land from his volcanoes. Those hearty enough to survive such outbursts might find the cooled rivers turned into black glass that can be used for weapons and tools. Families devout to The Devouring Flame might feed their dead to him, casting the remains into a volcano's caldera in hopes of easing their fiery god's temper.

Kuu... just about everyone seems to forget Kuu, but that is simply Kuu's way. The Empty Vessel encourages their followers to take time to sit under a palm tree or up on a mountain rock and just be. In their presence, residents of the Indigo Isles can feel the expanse of existence and their place in it, which makes them realize how big they must seem to all the tiny fish in the ocean. In the void of Kuu, they can find wisdom and understanding.

Birth of the Eld

In the beginning, there was Nothing. Nothing was an empty void, devoid of any of the Elements that make up the world around us. But even from Nothing, the Eld was born. Was it gradually, or was it through one cataclysmic burst of energy? These are not questions we can ask, as this Nothing had no concept of space or time. So it was both, and it was neither.

Gradually and all at once, the void of Nothing became awash with the essence of the Eld. And from this, Nothing became Space, and from that moment, Time began. With these first two primordial Elements, our Universe was. Yet it was still an empty space. To our mortal conception of things, what had changed? We cannot say, as our minds can't comprehend a Nothing absent of either Space or Time, and Space, to us, may be as close as we can get to knowing Nothing. But we can say for certain that it did change.

From Space and Time, the other Elements began to form, filling the Space's emptiness with matter, thought, spirit, and even life. In what order did they form? We cannot know, as knowledge, memory, and comprehension are ever aspects of the Element of Mind, and these earliest eons existed before Mind. Even magic cannot answer these questions, for magic was formed of the Eld, weaved from its essences into all the various traditions with which we mortals meddle.

Perhaps they all formed together, or perhaps one at a time. Since Time existed, we can at least be certain that there is an answer, even if it's not an answer we can ever know. Whatever might be the case, we know that the Eld in our time is woven of twenty Elements, and we know that this has been true throughout all of recorded history, and to the extent that our magics can reveal the secrets of the past.

We also know that these Elements have never shifted in that same time frame. They are, as they have always been: Air, Body, Darkness, Death, Earth, Electricity, Fire, Force, Ice, Life, Light, Metal, Mind, Music, Poison, Space, Spirit, Time, Water, and Wood.

Tova, God of Law.name: 托娃，律法之神 Tova, God of Law
Tova, God of Law.text:
父亲

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.GI5OjG1dQjYXOiSr]{Tova}

阵营: LN（LG、LN、LE）

兄弟姐妹: 未知

神殿: 官僚建筑、法庭、卫兵哨站、图书馆

崇拜者: 立法者、政治家、建筑师

神圣动物: 羊驼

神圣颜色: 蓝配白

领域: 城市、知识、完美、真理

牧师法术: 1环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.rVANhQgB8Uqi9PTl]{Animate Rope}，5环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.m4Mc5XbdML92BKOE]{Strange Geometry}，8环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.uEyfLoFQsRKBRIcB]{Scintillating Pattern}

偏好武器: 臂铠

神圣能力: 智力或感知

神圣技能: 社会

神圣灌注: 伤害或治疗

关键典范: 尽你所能在任何地方创造秩序体系；遵守所有当地法律，即便只按字面执行；为自己创造一套强而有力的道德准则

关键禁忌: 明知故犯地违法；在有组织的系统中创造混沌；故意违抗社会期望

父亲通常就以“父亲”之名为人所知，但有时也被称为托瓦（Tova）。他也被称作授法者与不可移动之物体。有些人认为父亲是法律与秩序的化身，但这究竟意味着他仁善还是邪恶，则取决于个人观点。尽管他的权能与目标与母亲相悖，他们仍保持着相对合作的关系；然而，当他们在某事上意见相左时，秩序与混沌的力量便会碰撞，并在世界上制造纷争。

第一次这样的动荡，发生在塔利尔（Talir）被囚禁之后不久。莉琳（Lilin）煽动她的新子嗣在世界上释放力量，猛烈冲撞长者（The Eld），从而引发了元素大灾变（The Calamity of Elements）。父亲调停了元素能量之间的争端，并帮助平息事态。父母之间第二次重大分歧导致阿拉卡尔（Alacar）各地战火纷起，常被称为刀刃大灾变（The Calamity of Blades）。在将近二十年的流血与战斗之后，父母再度携手，又孕育了两个能够为战争带来平衡的孩子：扎特拉（Zatera）与瑟梅莱（Semelleh）。

父亲与其他守法心性的孩子相处融洽，尤其是负责维持礼仪的卡斯蒂利（Castilli），以及负责记录与追踪阿拉卡尔诸多历史的赫斯特（Herst）。他往往在世界的日常事务上退居幕后，让他所启迪的体系维持阿拉卡尔相对稳健的进步与成长。父亲对他与母亲共同塑造的世界充满信心，也为近来的黄金时代感到自豪，即便危险渐长的迹象已然显现。

托瓦的追随者遍布世界各地，尤其常见于城市、军事设施，以及任何需要或崇尚秩序之处。其神职人员往往兼任政治家、公职人员、卫兵与士兵，但他们也能在会计、建筑师、图书馆员，甚至码头工人等职业中找到成就感。凡是需要一套系统、或偏好组织化的地方，仅仅进行安排与结构化的行为本身，就已是在赞颂托瓦。

即便是为父亲增光的艺术与建筑作品，也会运用黄金比例与神圣几何。大多数父亲的神殿都会在中央集会厅设有一处回声廊（whispering gallery）。而较新建成的神殿则开始加入复杂的发条装置，使神殿的整间房室都能变形为新的布局。

最虔诚的信徒往往研读托瓦的圣典《算法与性相》（Algorithms and Aspects）。该书探讨系统如何在日常生活中运作，并提出创造平衡市政结构的建议，使其能服务于最多的人。这部巨著并不列出具体法律，而是讲述如何在文明的需求与不同治理方式的现实性之间进行权衡。在其他章节中，它也谈及如何在个人生活中实现平衡与和谐。

托瓦的祭司常会成为其社群的领袖，在将《算法与性相》中的知识尽数汲取之后，再把这些感悟应用于塑造当地政府。另一些人则采取更个人化的方式，充当分析师，与个体合作，以一种自律而有意义的方式将其生活中的混沌纳入掌控。

父母

他们是最先抵达阿拉卡尔的存在，其动机未知。也许他们选择这个世界作为建立其家族的起点，仅仅是出于某种帮助凡人的内在需求——正如他们各自的教会所宣称的那样。又或许他们别有更自私的目的。由于他们神性超凡，凡人或许永远无法真正知晓答案。

无论如何，他们将长久留在阿拉卡尔，繁衍出强大的后裔来引导并塑造世界，同时始终维持平衡。

The Father

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.Item.GI5OjG1dQjYXOiSr]{Tova}

Alignment: LN (LG, LN, LE)

Siblings: Unknown

Temples: bureaucratic buildings, courts, guard outposts, libraries

Worshippers: lawmakers, politicians, architects

Sacred Animal: alpaca

Sacred Colors: blue with white

Domains: cities, knowledge, perfection, truth

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.rVANhQgB8Uqi9PTl]{Animate Rope}, 5th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.m4Mc5XbdML92BKOE]{Strange Geometry}, 8th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.uEyfLoFQsRKBRIcB]{Scintillating Pattern}

Favored Weapon: gauntlet

Divine Ability: Intelligence or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Society

Divine Font: harm or heal

Key Edicts: create systems of order wherever you can, obey all local laws even if only to the letter, create for yourself a strong moral code

Key Anathema: knowingly break laws, create chaos in an organized system, intentionally defy social expectations

The Father often just goes by the name of "The Father," but also sometimes goes by the name Tova. He is also known as Lawbringer and the Immovable Object. Some consider the Father the epitome of law and order, but whether that makes him benevolent or malicious depends upon one's point of view. Though his portfolio and goals run counter to The Mother, they share a relatively cooperative relationship; however, when they disagree on something the powers of order and chaos clash and create strife in the world.

The first such disturbance happened immediately following the imprisonment of Talir. Lilin incited her new children to unleash their power on the world, butting up sharply against The Eld and causing The Calamity of Elements. The Father mediated the dispute between the elemental energies and helped calm things down. The second major disagreement between The Parents caused several wars to break out all over Alacar, often called The Calamity of Blades. After almost twenty years of bloodshed and fighting, The Parents came together and had two more children that could give Balance to wars: Zatera and Semelleh.

The Father gets along with other lawful minded children, especially Castilli who enforces decorum and Herst who records and tracks the many histories of Alacar. He tends to take a back seat on the day-to-day happenings in the world, allowing the systems he has helped inspire to keep Alacar's progress and growth relatively steady. The Father is confident of the world he has helped shape with The Mother, and he is proud of the recent golden age, even as signs show danger growing.

Tova's followers can be found around the world, particularly in cities, military installations, and anywhere that order is needed or revered. Clergy tend to double as politicians, public officials, guards, and soldiers, but they can also find fulfillment in occupations such as accountants, architects, librarians, and even dock workers. Anywhere a system is needed, or organization is preferred, the very act of arranging and structuring gives praise to Tova.

Even works of art and architecture that give glory to The Father utilize golden ratios and sacred geometry. Most temples to The Father feature a whispering gallery in their central meeting chamber. More newly built temples have been incorporating complicated clockwork devices that allow for the transformation of entire rooms of the temple into new configurations.

The most devout tend to study Tova's holy book, Algorithms and Aspects, which discusses the functions of systems on everyday life and contains recommendations for creating balanced civic structures that can serve the largest number of people. The tome doesn't list specific laws, but talks about how to weigh the needs of a civilization against the practicalities of different ways of governing. In other chapters, it also talks about achieving balance and harmony in an individual's life.

Priests of Tova often become leaders in their community, learning what they can from Algorithms and Aspects before applying that wisdom to shape their local governments. Others take a much more personal approach, and function as analysts who work with individuals on bringing the chaos of their lives under control in a disciplined and meaningful way.

The Parents

The first to arrive on Alacar, their motives are unknown. Perhaps they have chosen this world to start their Family simply due to some intrinsic need to help mortals, as their respective churches claim. Or perhaps they have some other, more selfish purpose. As they are divine, mortals may never truly know the answer.

Nevertheless they are here to stay on Alacar, spawning powerful progeny who guide and shape the world, but always maintaining The Balance.

Lilin, God of Chaos.name: 莉琳，混沌之神 Lilin, God of Chaos
Lilin, God of Chaos.text:
母亲

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.zPlUgqHJ7U24Rahe]{Lilin}

阵营： CN（CG、CN、CE）

兄弟姐妹： 未知

神殿： 艺术工作室、废品场、木材场、工坊

崇拜者： 艺术家、爆破手、表演者、拾荒者

圣兽： 蝴蝶

圣色： 蓝白相间

领域： 创造、毁灭、家庭、隐秘

牧师法术： 1环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.r8g7oSumKOHDqJsd]{Agitate}，4环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.TUj8eugNqAvB1vVR]{Creation}，6环：@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]{Disintegrate}

偏好武器： 战斗扇

神圣能力： 感知或魅力

神圣技能： 制作或表演

神圣灌注： 伤害或治疗

关键典范： 创造新事物、煽动改变、留下传承

关键禁忌： 保护机构免于改变、任由自己停滞、袖手旁观

母亲常常就以“母亲”之名自称，但有时也会被称作莉琳。她也被称为“变革的勇士”与“不可阻挡的力场”。母亲代表着世界中的混沌——既是毁灭的手段，也是创造的手段。对她而言，停滞才是唯一真正的罪，因为我们应当永远追求自我提升、追求创造，并在阿拉卡尔留下未来世代不会遗忘的印记。

也正因如此，在各种故事中，母亲既被塑造成英雄，也被塑造成恶徒。父亲坚定不移，有时甚至会显得专横或略带威权；而母亲在神话中的角色，则会因讲述者的立场不同而改变。当她与父亲将塔利尔封锁起来时，她的愤怒与悲伤在阿拉卡尔引发了大灾难。但也正是由于母亲的介入，伟大的艺术作品才得以启发、经典交响曲才得以谱写、优美的诗篇才得以写就。创造出崭新之物、恒久之物，便是对母亲的赞颂；因为她激励一切凡人攀登新的高峰，去做那些他们从未想过自己能做到的事。也因此，大多数吟游诗人与工匠会在演出与展览开始前，向作为“伟大缪斯”的莉琳祈祷。

对母亲的崇拜在音乐厅与美术馆中最为盛大，这些地方几乎总会设有一处神社，让参与者留下自己的印记，或以某种方式改变它。这可能简单到一本供来宾签名的留言簿；也可能是一堆黏土，人人都来帮忙塑形，直到活动结束后将黏土烧制成一座新雕像，适合摆放在喷泉旁或市集广场上。另一些人则只是与酒馆的吟游诗人一起歌唱来崇拜她，或在做午饭前赞颂她。烹饪是对母亲强有力的赞美形式，因为烹饪这一行为本身会让食物发生转变。

因此，莉琳对世界的影响是持续而细微的：存在于世间每一次创造与毁灭之中，也存在于一切塑造并改变世界的事物之中。她没有一本严苛的圣典可供人宣讲经文，因为赞歌很快就会变得陈腐并失去意义。虔诚的母亲信徒有时会携带一叠牌卡，每张牌上都有一幅艺术作品。他们洗牌并发牌，试图占卜每一张牌背后的含义，以及它与自己当下生活时刻的关联。也有人用纸上涂抹的墨迹飞溅，或将覆满符文的骰子掷入石碗。这些都映照出这位女神反复无常、永恒变化的本性。

同样地，她的圣地也总在变化。永久性的母亲神殿十分罕见。相反，大多数献给莉琳的神殿都是当时创造出来的，或为某个特定目的而搭建的。比如在盛大的月之庆典期间，有些莉琳的追随者每年都会为母亲搭起一座帐篷，但会选在聚落中不同的地点，而且每次内部都会展示不同的本地艺术家作品。

同理，母亲几乎不存在正式的教会层级结构。各个神职群体都会以自己的方式崇拜莉琳，并创造出各种本地习俗。这些习俗往往会随着新风尚、观念传播与时尚更迭而年年改变。追随母亲教诲的人不仅是艺术家，也包括政治活动家、革命者，甚至爆破手。毕竟，让世界发生改变，既可以以毁灭的形式出现，也可以以创造的形式出现。

秩序与混沌

双亲处于相对的和谐之中，尽管他们代表着完全的对立面，人们或许会以为他们必然彼此敌对。这两股力量相互抵消，而这正是构成整个“平衡”的核心，也是“家族”得以形成的基础。

但事情并非总是如此。在阿拉卡尔历史上的某些时期，两者曾势同水火，这总会给凡人带来纷争。如今他们以和谐的协作共存，也许是因为他们明白：彻底的支配最终会以灾害告终。

The Mother

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.zPlUgqHJ7U24Rahe]{Lilin}

Alignment: CN (CG, CN, CE)

Siblings: Unknown

Temples: art studios, scrap yards, lumberyards, workshops

Worshippers: artists, demolitionists, performers, salvagers

Sacred Animal: butterfly

Sacred Colors: white with blue

Domains: creation, destruction, family, secrecy

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.r8g7oSumKOHDqJsd]{Agitate}, 4th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.TUj8eugNqAvB1vVR]{Creation}, 6th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]{Disintegrate}

Favored Weapon: fighting fan

Divine Ability: Wisdom or Charisma

Divine Skill: Crafting or Performance

Divine Font: harm or heal

Key Edicts: create something new, incite change, leave a legacy

Key Anathema: protect institutions from change, allow yourself to stagnate, stand on the sidelines

The Mother often just goes by the name of The Mother, but also sometimes goes by the name Lilin. She is also known as the Champion of Change and the Unstoppable Force. The Mother represents chaos in the world, as both a means of destruction and creation. For her, stagnation is the only true sin as we should always be looking to better ourselves, to create, and to make a mark on Alacar that future generations will not forget.

It is for these reasons that The Mother is cast as both a hero and a villain in various stories. While The Father is steadfast and sometimes overbearing or slightly authoritarian, The Mother's role in myth changes depending on the point of view of whomever is telling the story. When she and The Father locked Talir away, her rage and sorrow caused calamity on Alacar. But it is also for The Mother's intervention that great works of art are inspired, classic symphonies written, and melodic poetry written. To create something new, something lasting, is praise to The Mother, as she inspires all mortals to reach new heights and do things they had never thought possible. For this reason, most bards and artisans open their shows and exhibits with a prayer to Lilin as the Great Muse.

Worship of The Mother is at its grandest in concert halls and art galleries, which almost always include a shrine for attendees to leave their own mark, or change it in some way. This might be as simple as a guest book to sign, or a mound of clay that everyone helps to sculpt until the event is finished and the clay fired into a new statue, fit for a fountain or market square. Others worship her simply through singing with a tavern bard or praise her before making lunch. Cooking is a strong form of praise to The Mother because the act of cooking is transformative to the food itself.

Therefore the influence Lilin has on the world is constant and small, in every act of creation and destruction in the world and through everything that shapes and changes it. There is no rigorous holy book from which to spout scripture, for the psalms would soon become stale and lose meaning. Rather than a holy book, devout of The Mother sometimes carry packs of cards with artwork on each card. They shuffle and deal the cards, trying to divine the meaning behind each one as it relates to that moment in their lives. Others use splashes of ink smeared on paper or rune-covered dice tossed about a stone bowl. These all reflect the capricious and ever-changing nature of the goddess.

In much the same way, her holy places are always changing as well. Permanent temples to The Mother are rare. Instead, most temples to Lilin are created contemporaneously, or erected for a specific purpose. For instance, during the Grand Lunar Festival, some followers of Lilin raise a tent to The Mother each year, but on a different spot in their settlement, and each time featuring different local artists in the interior.

Similarly, an official church hierarchy of The Mother is virtually non-existent. Instead, each group of clergy worships Lilin in their own way and create all sorts of their own local customs. These customs are often changed from year to year with new fads, the spreading of ideas, and updates to fashion. Adherents to The Mother's teachings are not just artists, but also political activists, revolutionaries, and even demolitionists. After all, making change in the world can take the form of destruction as easily as creation.

Law & Chaos

The parents are in relative harmony, even though they represent complete opposites and one might think that they would be opposed. The two forces neutralize each other, which is the core behind the entire Balance on which the Family is formed.

But this isn't always so. There are periods in Alacar's history where the two were diametrically opposed, which always led to strife for mortals. For now they exist in harmonious cooperation, perhaps knowing that complete dominion would end in disaster.

Aoz, God of Life.name: Aoz，生命之神 Aoz, God of Life
Aoz, God of Life.text:
火花

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.vX7EAGC2gbevLClI]{Aoz}

阵营: CN（CG、N、CN、CE）

兄弟姐妹: Zoa

神殿: 育婴所、德鲁伊圆环、疗养院

崇拜者: 医师、接生婆、德鲁伊、农夫、花匠

圣兽: 蠕虫

圣色: 白色与灰色

领域: 创造、梦境、治疗、太阳

牧师法术: 1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.K9gI08enGtmih5X1]{Protector Tree}，2环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.BBvV7qoXGdw09q1C]{Speak with Animals}，4环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.qvwIwJ9QBihy8R0t]{Speak with Plants}

偏好武器: 刃围巾

神圣能力: 体质或感知

神圣技能: 医学

神授魔池: 治疗

关键典范: 治疗病患、帮助创造生命、摧毁不死生物

关键禁忌: 故意杀死一名活的生物、阻止生命蓬勃生长

Aoz，有时也被称为“生命赐予者”，是所有生命的神祇。《均衡》诸篇宣称，他们的权能并不只涵盖有智生命或类人生物，而是涵盖阿拉卡尔中一切被视为“活”的事物——其中包括被称为vitae的、象征行星生命力的无实体化身。人们说，Aoz在他们的灵魂熔炉中辛勤劳作，创造新的灵魂，或重塑旧灵魂，以充盈世间的活之容器。Aoz即生命。对追随《均衡》之人而言，能使人复活的是Aoz的仁善；能让婴儿降生的是他们的感知；而万物得以生长繁盛，则仰赖他们的恩典。可以说，他们承担着阿拉卡尔最重要的职责之一：创造并守护生命。

每当一个婴儿降生于世，崇拜《均衡》的父母便向Aoz祈祷；每当从果园采下一颗健康的苹果，农夫便赞颂Aoz。

凡是崇拜《均衡》的主要城市，都能找到奉祀Aoz的神殿——至少也会有神社；许多城镇亦然。甚至有些小村落也会设立一尊雕像或一幅挂毯以侍奉Aoz。在这些圣所里，人们能获得治疗，有时还能见证奇迹。Aoz的祭司最可能出现在这些区域周边，随时准备接纳病患或伤者。他们与自己的神祇协力，为社群成员治疗、担任接生婆，并祝福附近植物的生长。

尽管大多数祭司倾向于帮助自己的社群，但也有人更喜欢旅行、游历世界，将Aoz的野性生长传播给所有人，而不只限于居住在神殿附近的人。这些人最有可能决定加入冒险队伍；然而他们“不杀任何活物”的神圣誓约，可能会让同伴在某些处境中倍感棘手。也有人将自己献身于清剿世间的不死生物，作为幽魂与巫妖的猎手而行动。在不死横行的沙漠里，Aoz的祭司会带队深入沙海，摧毁在其中筑巢的骸骨怪物，并试图让大地重获肥沃。

但教会的大多数人并没有如此好战；他们通常会分发《医者日志》的抄本——这是一部由和平年代某位著名外科医师撰写的、实用的类人生物医学指南。或者他们会发放《绿中缝纫》——本质上是一部农夫年鉴。这两本书构成了Aoz更为实用的典籍，而一位优秀的祭司总能在其指引的字里行间，为日常生活找到感知。关于生命本质的更具哲思的讨论，则可见于《生命之环》（Circulus Vitae）。

Aoz的祭司常与Zoa的祭司并肩合作；Zoa是Aoz的双生神祇，司掌死亡。当两位神祇的神殿同时存在时，这些神殿总是彼此为邻；若非如此，也往往同处一座建筑之中——因为两种宗教都相信生死之环，相信每一个新开端都源自另一个开端的终结。通常，每个宗教都会有一位祭司与另一位结成永久的羁绊，以映照他们神祇之间的羁绊。这种配对有时被称作“阴阳之对”（In-yō Pair），他们会在整个成年人生中携手，为社群或其他共同目标服务。尽管这类组合有时显得古怪，但当生命与死亡的和谐被共同运用时，所产生的效果极为巨大——正如Aoz与Zoa自身一般。

圆环

有时被称为“生命之环”，有时被称为“死亡之环”。这是一切活之事物都应遵循的原则：从出生到生命到死亡。为了生存，一切生命都取自另一种生命：吃肉、伐木取材，甚至素食者也会食用植物。

当某个生物违背这些法则——例如不死生物凭借死亡之力而“活”，或由奥术能量驱动的魔像——它们便是在冒犯圆环，因此也就与“双生子”为敌。

The Spark

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.vX7EAGC2gbevLClI]{Aoz}

Alignment: CN (CG, N, CN, CE)

Siblings: Zoa

Temples: nurseries, druidic circles, infirmaries

Worshippers: physicians, midwives, druids, farmers, florists

Sacred Animal: worm

Sacred Colors: white and gray

Domains: creation, dreams, healing, sun

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.K9gI08enGtmih5X1]{Protector Tree}, 2nd: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.BBvV7qoXGdw09q1C]{Speak with Animals}, 4th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.qvwIwJ9QBihy8R0t]{Speak with Plants}

Favored Weapon: bladed scarf

Divine Ability: Constitution or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Medicine

Divine Font: heal

Key Edicts: heal the ill, help create life, destroy undead

Key Anathema: kill a living creature intentionally, prevent life from flourishing

Aoz, known sometimes as Life Bringer, is the deity of all life. The texts of the Balance claim that they don't hold dominion over just sapient life or humanoids, but everything in all of Alacar considered to be "living," including the insubstantial embodiments of the planet's life force known as vitae. Folks say Aoz toils away at their Soul Forge creating new souls or re-shaping old ones to fill the living vessels of the world. Aoz is life. To those who follow the Balance, it is by Aoz's benevolence that a person can be resurrected, it is by their wisdom that a baby can be born, and it is by their grace every living thing can grow and thrive. Theirs is, arguably, one of the most important jobs in all of Alacar: to create and preserve life.

For every baby that is born into the world, parents who worship the Balance pray to Aoz. For every healthy apple picked from the orchard, the farmer praises Aoz.

Temples to Aoz, or at the very least shrines, can be found in every major city that worship the Balance and many towns as well. Even some small villages include a statue or tapestry in service to Aoz. At these holy places, one can find healing and sometimes miracles. Sacerdos of Aoz are most likely found around these areas, ready to receive the sick or injured. They work with their deity to heal members of their community, act as midwives, and bless the growth of nearby plant life.

Though most sacerdos tend to help their communities, others prefer to travel and see the world, spreading the wild growth of Aoz to all people, not just those who live near a temple. These are the most likely to decide to join an adventuring party, though their holy vow to kill no living thing can make some situations challenging for their friends. Others have dedicated themselves to the eradication of undead in the world, working as hunters of ghosts and liches. In deserts rife with undeath, Aoz's sacerdos leads excursions into the sands to destroy the skeletal monstrosities who nest there and try and bring fertility back to the land.

But most of the church is not as militant as that; they tend to hand out copies of A Medic's Journal, a practical guide to humanoid medicine written by a famous surgeon from the Age of Peace. Or they give out Sewing In Green, essentially a farmer's almanac. These two books comprise the more practical texts of Aoz, and a good sacerdos can always find wisdom for everyday life in the subtext of their directions. More philosophical discussions on the nature of life can be found in the Circulus Vitae.

Aoz's sacerdos often work hand in hand with the sacerdos of Zoa, Aoz's twin deity of death. When both deities' temples exist, the temples are always neighboring, if not within the same edifice, since both religions believe in the circle of life and death, and that every new beginning comes from some other beginning's end. Often, a sacerdos from each religion will form a permanent bond with the other, mirroring the bond of their deities. This pairing, sometimes called an In-yō Pair, work together for their entire adult lives to serve their communities or other common goal. Though they may sometimes create an odd coupling, the harmony of life and death has great effects when applied together, much like Aoz and Zoa themselves.

The Circle

Sometimes called The Circle of Life, other times the Circle of Death. This is the principle that all living things should follow: birth to life to death. To survive, all life takes from other life: eating meat, chopping down trees for lumber, even vegetarians eat plants.

When a creature breaks these laws, such as undead "living" from the power of death or a golem powered by arcane energies, they are an affront to The Circle, and therefore make enemies of The Twins.

Zoa, God of Death.name: Zoa，死亡之神 Zoa, God of Death
Zoa, God of Death.text:
帷幕

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.tf9JxXmzcp1EtRXl]{Zoa}

阵营： LN（LG、LN、N、LE）

手足： Aoz

神殿：墓地、停尸间、战场、坟墓

崇拜者：殡葬师、掘墓人、士兵

圣兽：毛毛虫

圣色：黑与灰

领域：黑暗、死亡、命运、梦魇

牧师法术：1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.k34hDOfIIMAxNL4a]{Grim Tendrils}，3环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.AMEu5zzLN7uCX645]{Ghostly Weapon}，8环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Ht35SDf9PDStJfoC]{Spirit Song}

偏好武器：镰刀

神圣能力：体质或感知

神圣技能：宗教

神授源泉：伤害

关键典范：尊重死者与其安息之所，接受死亡的必然性，摧毁不死生物

关键禁忌：让死者复生，创造不死生物，打扰死者的休息

“死亡降临于所有人，”人们这么说，而对于平衡的追随者而言，事实也正是如此：Zoa最终会将所有生命都纳入其支配之下，甚至包括巨龙的生命。他们在Alacar的力量极其强大，但那是一种沉睡的力量、耐心的力量；因为Zoa在隧道尽头的光里等待着，准备摘取那些逝去之人的灵魂，并将其带往……没有任何凡人能确切知晓。有些平衡的宗教学者宣称，死者的灵魂会被交给Aoz，由其在灵魂熔炉中重新塑形，再次置入世界。也有人声称灵魂会被Zoa吞噬，被一口吃下；对那些过着充实而精彩人生的凡人而言，甚至还会发出一种多汁的“啵”的声响。

不追随平衡者则不相信Zoa对生与死有如此之大的影响力，并指出在Aoz与Zoa诞生之前，生物早已生生死死。无论真相如何，似乎没人能确知——或者至少，即使有人知道，他们也不会说出来……

不管怎样，他们并非邪恶的神祇。Zoa与其追随者明白：每一种活着的事物都会迎来终结，而对此自有一套体系来处理。当灵魂未能“前行”时，它们可能会化作不死生物；而对这对双子而言，没有什么比不死生物更是冒犯。幽灵、怨灵与喧灵尚未回归伟大的灵体平衡；而具有智能、有实体的不死生物仍滞留在其血肉容器之中，未能如其所应在死亡中前行；至于无心智的不死生物，则只是被赋予动作的血肉，抓住那本不再属于它们的生命不放。

尤其好战的阴阳对可能会将时间用于在各处搜寻并摧毁不死生物。Zoa的sacerdos在不进行丧葬安排、或照料死者的安息之所时，常常会制作并准备用来对付不死生物的武器。

许多城镇与城市都设有一座供奉Zoa的神殿，人们在其中举行葬礼、处理遗骸，并让死者安息。sacerdos守护这些神圣之地，确保死者得以持续休息。当聚落中有人报告闹鬼或其他诡异事件时，他们也会登门处理。因此，若冒险者热衷于猎杀不死生物，Zoa的神殿会是寻找工作的好去处。

有些Zoa的sacerdos也会举行降神会与其他仪式，让活人得以联系他们已逝的朋友与亲人。不同教派对这些仪式的本质与是否妥当各有看法，但能够施行这些圣礼本身就暗示：Zoa并不介意与亡者沟通。这些仪式记载于《Circulus Mortis》——Zoa的宗教典籍——而他们的sacerdos则通过此书学习如何处理各种类型的遗骸。见习者的首要职责之一，就是将此书从头到尾背诵下来，然后向一位年长的sacerdos复诵；这教会他们直面死亡时所需的耐心，并确保他们具备从事该职业的性情。毕竟，“死亡终将降临于所有人，随着时间推移。”

生命 & 死亡

Aoz与Zoa在所有事情上看起来几乎都很被动，只是以一种冷淡、无情绪的态度观察着。这常常令其他手足困惑；他们似乎很少能理解Aoz与Zoa的动机，但这两位却总是完美同步。

这对双子——人们常这样称呼他们——很少开口；而一旦开口，往往也是简短而断续的句子，声音几乎带着乐音般的质感。手足之间流传着传闻：Aoz与Zoa能够彼此通过心灵感应交流。

尽管其他孩子没有任何人会在公开场合承认，但Aoz与Zoa有时被认为是家族中最强大、也最神秘的孩子。

The Veil

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.tf9JxXmzcp1EtRXl]{Zoa}

Alignment: LN (LG, LN, N, LE)

Siblings: Aoz

Temples: graveyards, mortuaries, battlefields, tombs

Worshippers: morticians, gravediggers, soldiers

Sacred Animal: caterpillar

Sacred Colors: black and gray

Domains: darkness, death, fate, nightmares

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.k34hDOfIIMAxNL4a]{Grim Tendrils}, 3rd: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.AMEu5zzLN7uCX645]{Ghostly Weapon}, 8th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Ht35SDf9PDStJfoC]{Spirit Song}

Favored Weapon: sickle

Divine Ability: Constitution or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Religion

Divine Font: harm

Key Edicts: respect the dead and their resting place, accept the inevitability of death, destroy undead

Key Anathema: return the dead to life, create undead, disturb the dead's rest

"Death comes to all," as they say, and so it is that to followers of the Balance, Zoa claims all life under their dominion eventually, even the lives of dragons. Their power on Alacar is great, but it is a sleeping power, a patient power, for Zoa waits in the light at the end of the tunnel, ready to pluck out the souls of those who perish and bring them... no mortal knows for certain. Some religious scholars of the Balance claim the souls of the dead are given to Aoz to reshape on their Soul Forge and place into the world again. Others claim they're consumed by Zoa, eaten whole with a juicy sounding pop for mortals that have lived full and interesting lives.

Those who don't follow The Balance don't believe that Zoa has so much influence over life and death, pointing out that creatures lived and died long before Aoz and Zoa were born. Whatever the case, no one seems to know for sure, or at least if they do, they aren't talking...

Regardless, they're not a malevolent deity. Zoa and their followers know that life ends for every living thing and there is a system in place for dealing with that. When souls don't pass on, they can turn into undead, and there's nothing more of an affront to the twins than undead. Ghosts, specters, and poltergeists haven't returned to the Great Spiritual Balance, while intelligent, corporeal undead have remained in their fleshy vessels and not passed on in death, as they should, and mindless undead are simply animated flesh clinging to life that no longer belongs to them.

Particularly combat minded In-yō Pairs might spend their time hunting and destroying undead wherever they can find them. When sacerdos of Zoa are not making funerary arrangements or tending to the resting places of the dead, they are often creating and preparing weapons to use against the undead.

Many towns and cities have a temple to Zoa where they hold funerals, prepare remains, and lay the dead to rest. The sacerdos look over these sacred places, ensuring the dead are allowed to stay at rest. They also make house calls when someone in the settlement reports a haunting or other strange occurrence. As such, temples of Zoa are good places for adventurers to look for work if they're keen on hunting the undead.

Some sacerdos of Zoa also perform seances and other rituals to allow the living to contact their dead friends and relatives. Different sects have varying opinions on both the nature and appropriateness of these rituals, but the very ability to perform these rites suggests that Zoa doesn't mind communication with the deceased. The rituals are outlined in Circulus Mortis, the religious text of Zoa, and their sacerdos use this text to learn how to deal with various types of remains. One of the first duties of an acolyte is to memorize the book from cover to cover, then recite it for an elder sacerdos; this teaches them the patience required when staring in the face of death and ensures they have the temperament for the occupation. After all, "Death comes to all, in time."

Life & Death

Aoz and Zoa appear almost passive in all things and simply observe with a dispassionate and unemotional attitude. This often confounds the other Siblings, and they rarely seem to understand Aoz and Zoa's motives, but the two seem to always be in perfect sync.

The twins, as they are often called, rarely speak; when they do, it's often in short, halting sentences, with their voices almost sounding musical. There are rumors among the Siblings that Aoz and Zoa can communicate through telepathy with one another.

Although none of the other Children would ever admit it publicly, Aoz and Zoa are sometimes considered the most powerful,and mysterious, Children in The Family.

Zatera, God of War.name: 扎泰拉，战争之神 Zatera, God of War
Zatera, God of War.text:
血腥将军

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.s2g5CAX83ThCEy2C]{Zatera}

阵营: NE（N、LE、NE、CE）

手足: Semelleh

神殿: 战场、军事营地、锦标赛

崇拜者: 士兵、铸兵匠、商人

圣兽: 猛禽

圣色: 银与红

领域: 野心、强力、毁灭、热忱

牧师法术: 1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.jsWthW1Qy6tg3SwD]{Draw Ire}，2环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.hoR6w8BqX2F35Tdx]{Blistering Invective}，4环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.8M03UxGXjYyDFAoy]{Weapon Storm}

偏好武器: 长矛

神圣能力: 力量或体质

神圣技能: 威吓

神圣恩赐: 伤害

关键典范: 为你所信之物而战，夺取你应得之物，以智胜敌

关键禁忌: 对对手手下留情，在你正确时仍选择屈服，自愿逃跑

冲突与纷争会降临所有人；但当事态变得暴烈时，战斗女主便沉醉于毁灭与嗜血之中。扎泰拉仅仅是现身，就会在人们心中搅起暴力与攻击性，使邻里相斗、让两个长期结盟的国家也彼此以武力相威胁。当剑刃出鞘，扎泰拉的乐子才真正开始。她既享受酒馆斗殴的混沌，也同样享受国与国战争中那繁复的策略；而且她乐于挑起其中任何一种。扎泰拉认为自己在阿拉卡尔的职能，是帮助释放隐藏的压力与秘密的怨恨，把那些冲突带到台面上来。她也明白暴力往往是生存的一部分，并相信沉溺其中就是接纳我们天生的冲动。

扎泰拉的神职人员以军事头衔组织：将军、中将、少将，等等。他们常充当大国与强大城邦的军事顾问，影响其外交政策与边境防务上的决策。阿拉卡尔最优秀的一些军事学院，便由扎泰拉教会经营。

当真正的战争爆发时，扎泰拉的势力总会出现，为战略上较弱的一方提供他们的服务，充当武器掮客与雇佣兵——因为没有什么比确保一场漫长而有趣的战争更好的方式了。屠杀对血腥将军毫无兴趣；但一场会战，其结果取决于将领的狡计与士兵的技能？那才是值得她称颂的盛事。

扎泰拉之所以邪恶，是因为她相信以血淋淋的技艺对决为荣，并对过程中发生的生命损失抱持漫不经心的态度；而不是因为她为了屠戮与死亡本身而渴求它们。只要能打得漂亮并取胜，她也不介意某个下属找到办法饶敌不死。

在和平时期，扎泰拉的军官会不断训练新的崇拜者、打造武器，并偶尔举行模拟战。这些活动用以磨砺指挥官的战略与士兵的技能。重演著名战役也是教会的消遣之一，不过他们使用真钢利刃，有时会造成少量伤亡。

虔信者以及军事学院学生会阅读若干书籍、卷轴与石板，上面概述了扎泰拉所重视的各类战斗风格与策略。尽管她并没有一本所谓的“圣典”，《战争的目的》是一部关于战斗与暴力的哲学讨论，且常常成为扎泰拉军官之间神学讨论的主要来源。

至于这位女神，她行走于世界之上，所至之处皆点燃暴力，并沉醉于其中的神圣真理。她不愿被关在兵营或作战室里，反而喜爱身处前线：在泥泞营地里入睡，行军时啃着口粮，不知疲倦地连续跋涉数小时。当她旅行时，她喜欢变换形态以融入当地人群；没人敢反对血浸之刃，但他们也许会与某个来到城里、压低他们价格的竞争商人狠狠干上一架。凡有争斗与纷争之处，你都能找到扎泰拉的足迹——这也使她成为阿拉卡尔最为活跃的神祇之一。

战争与和平

扎泰拉与Semelleh如同神性层面的一对手足：一个想玩正面对决的棋盘游戏，另一个只想坐着读书。两者相处得出奇融洽，但兴趣不同，而Semelleh只希望他的妹妹放下凡人，别再胡闹。

Bloody General

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.s2g5CAX83ThCEy2C]{Zatera}

Alignment: NE (N, LE, NE, CE)

Siblings: Semelleh

Temples: battlefields, military encampments, tournaments

Worshippers: soldiers, weaponsmiths, merchants

Sacred Animal: raptor

Sacred Colors: silver and red

Domains: ambition, might, destruction, zeal

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.jsWthW1Qy6tg3SwD]{Draw Ire}, 2nd: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.hoR6w8BqX2F35Tdx]{Blistering Invective}, 4th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.8M03UxGXjYyDFAoy]{Weapon Storm}

Favored Weapon: longspear

Divine Ability: Strength or Constitution

Divine Skill: Intimidation

Divine Font: harm

Key Edicts: fight for what you believe in, take what you deserve, outwit your enemies

Key Anathema: go easy on an opponent, yield when you are right, willingly flee

Conflict and strife come to all, but when things get violent, the Mistress of Battle revels in the destruction and bloodlust. Zatera's mere presence stirs up violence and aggression in people, causing neighbor to fight neighbor and two long allied countries to threaten violence. When the swords become unsheathed, that's when the fun really begins for Zatera. She enjoys the chaos of a bar fight as much as the complicated strategy of countries at war, and will gladly start either. Zatera sees her function on Alacar as helping to relieve the hidden pressures and secret resentments, bringing those conflicts out into the open. She also understands that violence is often part of survival, and believes reveling in it is accepting our natural urges.

Zatera's clergy are organized with military titles: general, lieutenant general, major general, and so on. They often act as military advisors to large nations and powerful city states, influencing their decisions on foreign policy and border defenses. Some of the best military academies in all of Alacar are run by the church of Zatera.

When a real war breaks out, Zatera's forces are always there offering their services as weapon brokers and mercenaries to the strategically weaker side, for there's no better way to ensure a long and interesting war. Slaughter holds no interest for the Bloody General, but a pitched battle whose outcome depends on the cunning of the generals and skill of the soldiers? That is an event worthy of her praise.

Zatera is evil because she believes in bloody contests of skill with a casual disregard for the loss of life that occurs in the process, not because she desires slaughter and death for their own purposes. She wouldn't mind a subordinate finding a way to spare enemies' lives, as long as they wage war brilliantly and win.

In times of peace Zatera's officers continually train new worshipers, make weapons, and sometimes hold mock battles. These serve to hone the strategies of her commanders and the skills of her soldiers. Recreating famous battles is also a pastime of the church, though they use live steel which sometimes results in a few casualties.

The devout, as well as military academy students, read the several books, scrolls, and tablets that outline the various fighting styles and strategies that are important to Zatera. Though she doesn't have a single "holy book" to speak of, The Purpose of War is a philosophical discussion on fighting and violence and often acts as the primary source of theological discussion amongst Zatera's officers.

As for the goddess, she walks the world, starting violence wherever she goes and reveling in the divine truth of it all. Not one to be cooped up in a barracks or war room, she enjoys being in the field, sleeping in muddy encampments and eating rations while marching tirelessly for hours. While she travels, she enjoys changing forms to blend in with the local populace; no one dares oppose the Bloodsoaked Blade but they might get in a fight with a rival merchant who has come to town and is undercutting their prices. Anywhere there's fighting and strife, you'll find the footprints of Zatera, which makes her one of the most prolific deities in all of Alacar.

War & Peace

Zatera and Semelleh are the divine equivalent of two siblings where one wants to play a head-to-head board game and the other just wants to sit and read. The two get along surprisingly well, but they have different interests, and Semelleh just wants his sister to set down the mortals and stop messing around.

Semelleh, God of Peace.name: 塞梅莱赫，和平之神 Semelleh, God of Peace
Semelleh, God of Peace.text:
温柔之手

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.ogEabbYqS8usPQKp]{Semelleh}

阵营: NG（LG、NG、CG、N）

兄弟姐妹: Zatera

神殿: 战场、自然保护区、大使馆

崇拜者: 外交官、谈判者、商人

圣兽: 树懒

圣色: 白配绿

领域: 自由、治疗、守护、完美

牧师法术: 1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.vLA0q0WOK2YPuJs6]{Charm}，2环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.0qaqksrGGDj74HXE]{Glitterdust}，8环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Oj1PJBMQD9vuwCv7]{Maze}

偏好武器: 拳

神圣能力: 感知或魅力

神圣技能: 交涉

神圣恩赐: heal

关键典范: 怜悯身边之人、协作以达成目标、与他人妥协

关键禁忌: 主动挑起战斗、撕毁停战协议、挑衅他人

阿拉卡尔的一句古老谚语说：“人人都渴望和平，直到出现只能靠战争才能得到的东西。”塞梅莱的崇拜亦是如此。阿拉卡尔的大多数人都知道这位神圣外交官；当战争的威胁仍在遥远之处时，他们往往只是口头敷衍地敬奉他。然而，当战争的阴影笼罩某个社群时，临时的神社与服务便会突然冒出来，向塞梅莱祈祷，让冲突远离他们的聚居地。对大多数人而言，他是“方便之神”；而对武器匠与渴望建功立业的野心士兵来说，他则是令人沮丧的障碍。

对外交官而言，他是一盏指引之光，是战争余波中毁灭喧嚣之上的理性之声。许多外交官崇拜塞梅莱，而虔诚到加入其教会者被称为“仲裁者”。一些仲裁者会在城镇与大型城市建立会馆，另一些则更喜欢在乡野间旅行，将善行与慈善传播到偏远的乡村城镇与聚居地。

依其教诲，仲裁者是和平主义者，主张以妥协与相互尊重来解决争端。当分歧演变为暴力时，他们会尽其所能维持和平，阻止流血，或至少避免生命的损失。正因如此，塞梅莱的教会几乎没有守护者；但那些决定以和平之名拿起武器的人，往往以无辜者的保护者身份行动，只与拒不退让者战斗。“温柔之手”的仲裁者使用非致命武器、法术与战术。他们会以特殊符文灌注他们的武器，以确保自己只是终结暴力，并给予侵略者一次悔改其暴行的机会。

当暴力结束后，仲裁者常会朗读《和平思考的七种方式》——塞梅莱最神圣的经典——以帮助人们学习以和平方式解决问题。这本书概述了解题技巧与谈判策略，使读者能够在不诉诸侵略的情况下达成目标。尽管书中充满华丽文辞、绚烂语言、隐喻与寓言，其中的训诫仍引导阿拉卡尔各处交战的国家建立起持久的和平条约。

塞梅莱的大多数圣日带有区域性质，用以庆祝某项和平条约签署的周年纪念日，或其他争端的终结。当然，这并不意味着阿拉卡尔的每个国家都会在每一场重大地方冲突结束时都庆祝塞梅莱——那很快就会变得难以为继。尽管如此，仲裁者仍会记录这些事件，并将其指定为较小的圣日。得益于近来阿拉卡尔世界的黄金年代，重大战争相对较少，因此塞梅莱新的主要区域性圣日也相对稀少；但Zatera一直在煽动战争，而全球范围内的冲突正在抬头。

塞梅莱本人在阿拉卡尔四处游历，尽可能帮助有需要的人，并在力所能及时阻止战争。他通常会以伪装的身份旅行，扮作日工、探险者或巡回医师。

他认为这些身份能让自己处于最适合帮助他人的位置。当他造访一个社群时，他会以慷慨与非侵略来终结纷争与暴力。他希望通过树立亲身榜样，培育一种社群意识，以及对他人福祉的个人责任感。

和平的代价

塞梅莱教导说，和平能为所有人带来繁荣；不过Zatera指出，这一教导并非总是正确，冲突也可能让人们团结起来并刺激经济。尽管如此，塞梅莱与其他喜欢轻松、宴饮与狂欢的“子嗣”关系良好，例如Amon、Ebrugeses与Chakisa。Zatera常以奢华的战争盛宴与胜利庆典，试图将这些神祇拉到自己一边。

The Gentle Hand

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.ogEabbYqS8usPQKp]{Semelleh}

Alignment: NG (LG, NG, CG, N)

Siblings: Zatera

Temples: battlefields, nature preserves, embassies

Worshippers: diplomats, negotiators, merchants

Sacred Animal: tree sloth

Sacred Colors: white with green

Domains: freedom, healing, protection, perfection

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.vLA0q0WOK2YPuJs6]{Charm}, 2nd: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.0qaqksrGGDj74HXE]{Glitterdust}, 8th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Oj1PJBMQD9vuwCv7]{Maze}

Favored Weapon: fist

Divine Ability: Wisdom or Charisma

Divine Skill: Diplomacy

Divine Font: heal

Key Edicts: have compassion for those around you, cooperate to achieve a goal, compromise with others

Key Anathema: initiate combat, break a truce, provoke another

An old Alacar proverb states, "All wish for peace until there is something they can only get by war." And so too is the worship of Semelleh. Most of the people of Alacar know about The Divine Diplomat and pay him lip service when the threat of war is a distant worry. However, when war looms over a community, impromptu shrines and services suddenly pop up, praying to Semelleh to keep the conflict away from their settlement. He is a god of convenience for most, a frustrating obstacle for weapon makers and ambitious soldiers wanting to prove themselves.

For diplomats, he is a guiding light, a voice of reason over the din of destruction that follows in war's wake. Many diplomats worship Semelleh, and those who are devout enough to join his church are known as Arbiters. While some Arbiters set up chapter houses in towns and large cities, others prefer to travel the countryside, spreading good works and charity to rural towns and settlements.

By their teachings, Arbiters are pacifists and look to settle disputes with compromise and mutual respect. When a disagreement turns violent, they try to keep the peace as best they can, preventing bloodshed or, at the very least, loss of life. Because of this, Semelleh's church contains few champions, but those who decide to take up arms in the name of peace often do so as protectors of the innocent, fighting only those who refuse to back down. Arbiters of The Gentle Hand use nonlethal weapons, spells, and tactics. They imbue their weapons with special runes to ensure they're only putting an end to the violence and giving aggressors another chance to repent their violent ways.

When the violence has ended, Arbiters often read from the Seven Ways of Peaceful Thinking, Semelleh's most holy text, in order to help people learn peaceful solutions to their problems. This book outlines problem solving techniques and negotiation strategies that can help the reader meet their objectives in place of the use of aggression. Though filled with flowery text, colorful language, metaphors, and parables, the lessons in the holy book have guided warring nations across Alacar to establish long lasting peace treaties.

Most of Semelleh's holy days are regional in nature, celebrating the anniversary of a peace treaty being signed, or some other dispute coming to an end. That's not to say that every nation on Alacar celebrates Semelleh on the ending of every major local conflict, though, as that would quickly become untenable. Nonetheless, Arbiters keep track of such things and designate them as minor holy days. Thanks to the recent golden age for the world of Alacar, there had been relatively few major wars, and thus relatively few new major regional holy days of Semelleh, but Zatera is always fomenting war and conflict is on the rise worldwide.

Semelleh himself roams Alacar, helping those in need and preventing war when he can. He usually travels in disguise as a day laborer, explorer, or traveling physician.

He finds these roles allow him to be in the best position to help those in need. While visiting a community, he helps to put an end to strife and violence through generosity and non-aggression. He hopes by setting a personal example, he can foster a sense of community and personal responsibility towards others' wellbeing.

The Wages of Peace

Semelleh teaches that peace brings prosperity for all, though Zatera points out that this teaching isn't always true and that conflict can bring people together and stimulate economy. Nonetheless, Semelleh has a good relationship with other Children who like to take it easy, feast, and party like Amon, Ebrugeses, and Chakisa. Zatera often seeks to get these deities on her side with lavish war banquets and victory celebrations.

Amon, God of Revelry.name: 阿蒙，狂欢之神 Amon, God of Revelry
Amon, God of Revelry.text:
派对之魂

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.krMugZsEmLD8LVGQ]{Amon}

阵营： CN（CG、CN、CE）

兄弟姊妹：卡斯提利

神殿：酒馆、剧院、音乐厅、酒吧

崇拜者：冒险者、初登社交界者、酒馆老板

圣兽：松鼠

圣色：棕色与金色

领域：自由、放纵、幸运、财富

牧师法术：1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.pBevG6bSQOiyflev]{Befuddle}，4环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.mUz3wqbMQ5JQ06M5]{Infectious Melody}，8环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Jvyy6oVIQsD34MHB]{Uncontrollable Dance}

偏好武器：链枷

神圣能力：体质或魅力

神圣技能：表演

神授源泉：伤害或治疗

关键典范：尽情享受、抽出时间放松、帮助他人获得乐趣

关键禁忌：逼迫他人放纵、毁掉派对、占醉酒者的便宜

派对、庆典与喧闹的酒馆，皆是阿蒙的领域。他依靠凡人放松并享受自我时迸发的鲜活能量而兴盛。阿蒙因常在气氛沉闷无聊的聚会中现身、为宾客注入活力，直到庆祝的人潮喧声震彻全场，而赢得了“派对之魂”的称号。在阿蒙未亲临的庆典上，若有格外欢愉的凡人同样带着这种富有感染力的喜悦，也可能被授予同样的称号，以此向这位纵乐之神致敬。

阿蒙喜爱玩耍、跳舞、歌唱与尽情欢乐，且常常会过度到近乎放纵的程度。因此，他几乎总由兄弟卡斯提利陪伴左右；后者会约束阿蒙，并试图阻止事态失控。即便如此，纵乐与混沌仍会伴随阿蒙所到之处的余波而来，这也令他乐在其中。看到凡人快乐，正是阿蒙活着的意义。

他的许多追随者也以同样的态度面对生活，竭力将幸福与无忧无虑的态度传播给所有相遇之人。阿蒙在阿拉卡各地的酒馆、宴饮厅与旅店中都受到赞颂。在特别喧闹的活动上，人们常会在尊位留下一把空椅，留给阿蒙，以备他决定现身。即便在贵族更正式、更优雅的社交场合，他们也会为阿蒙留椅；贵族们更娴熟、更讲究的庆祝方式，与醉醺醺的酒馆小调一样，都是对纵乐之神的崇拜。商船与海盗船上的水手也会在开桶劣酒时，为阿蒙举杯。

将一生奉献给追逐欢愉之道的人被称为“庆典者”。他们往往也兼任社群中的派对与活动策划者，借由社交享乐帮助人们找到幸福与团结。还有一些人则偏好更含蓄的方式：购置酒馆、舞厅或其他休闲场所，以传播阿蒙的喜乐。无论如何，他们的目标都是通过庆祝将社群凝聚在一起，令彼此之间建立更强的羁绊。

尽管庆典者并没有一本明确而权威的圣典可用来学习阿蒙的感知，但他们每个人都会记录自己的事迹日志，内容包括派对计划、可靠的葡萄酒商名单、各类吟游诗人与乐师的档期、鸡尾酒配方，有时还会记录适合畅饮的好歌的曲调与歌词。每位庆典者都会为自己的“文集”起一个名字，但往往会反映其内容的性质，例如 Portfunlio、Portefeuille Amusant，或干脆就叫 Fun Book。当庆典者相聚时，他们常会彼此分享信息，建立起自己的图书馆以汲取灵感。

每年一次，在迭吉娜的冬至，来自阿拉卡各地的庆典者会齐聚一堂，举办名为 Adin Noctis 的盛大庆典，其大意可译作“喧嚣之夜”，从黄昏持续到黎明。在这段时间里，庆典者会以掌声的音量提名并投票，选出一位新的“欢乐君主”，由其带领会众一年。这位“君主”作为教会的象征性领袖，协助决定在哪些地方需要更多代表，并发起任何特别项目，例如战后协助重建，或举办派对庆祝某国君主喜得新嗣。

纵乐与克制

这对兄弟姊妹（目前最常以兄弟的形象出现）象征着凡人之间的挣扎：要么放松过度直至懒散，要么维持一套无喜无乐、充满戒律与职责的日常。它们一同在阿拉卡旅行，各自试图说服对方其道路才是最佳选择，并常为与他们擦肩而过的凡人留下难忘的故事。当他们彼此平衡时，谁也算不上特别危险；但若只剩其一，便可能给凡人带来灾难。总体而言，当这对兄弟姊妹前来感召时，凡人往往会享受那段时光。

Life of the Party

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.krMugZsEmLD8LVGQ]{Amon}

Alignment: CN (CG, CN, CE)

Siblings: Castilli

Temples: taverns, theatres, concert halls, pubs

Worshippers: adventurers, debutants, tavern owners

Sacred Animal: squirrel

Sacred Colors: brown and gold

Domains: freedom, indulgence, luck, wealth

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.pBevG6bSQOiyflev]{Befuddle}, 4th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.mUz3wqbMQ5JQ06M5]{Infectious Melody}, 8th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Jvyy6oVIQsD34MHB]{Uncontrollable Dance}

Favored Weapon: flail

Divine Ability: Constitution or Charisma

Divine Skill: Performance

Divine Font: harm or heal

Key Edicts: indulge yourself, take time to relax, help others have fun

Key Anathema: pressure others into indulging, ruin a party, take advantage of the intoxicated

Parties, celebrations, and noisy taverns are Amon's domain. He thrives on the vibrant energy of mortals who relax and enjoy themselves. Amon has earned himself the title of Life of the Party through appearing at gatherings with a dull, boring vibe and energizing the guests until the throng of celebration can be heard throughout the event. At such celebrations where Amon is not present, particularly joyous mortals who carry a similar infectious mirth might also be given the same title, in honor of the god of revelry.

Amon likes to play, dance, sing, and be merry, often to the point of excess at times. This is why he is almost always accompanied by his brother, Castilli, who keeps Amon in check and tries to prevent things from getting out of hand. Nevertheless, revelry and chaos follow in the wake of Amon wherever he goes, much to his personal delight. Seeing mortals happy is what Amon lives for.

Many of his followers take this same attitude towards life, trying to spread happiness and a carefree attitude to all they meet. Amon is praised in taverns, feasthalls, and inns across Alacar. At particularly raucous events, it's not uncommon to leave an empty chair in a place of honor, reserved for Amon if he should decide to make an appearance. Even at more formal and elegant affairs of the aristocracy, they leave a chair for Amon; a noble's more practiced and nuanced celebration is just as much worship of the god of revelry as a drunken pub song. Sailors of merchant vessels and pirate ships alike raise a stein to Amon when they break out a barrel of swill.

Those who dedicate their life to this pursuit of mirth are called Celebrants. They often double as party and event planners in a community, helping people find happiness and togetherness through social enjoyment. Still others prefer a more subtle approach and purchase a tavern, dance hall, or other establishment of leisure to spread the joy of Amon. Nevertheless, their aim is to bring their communities together through celebration, creating stronger bonds between all.

While Celebrants don't have a single, definitive holy book to rely on learning Amon's wisdom, they do each keep a journal of their deeds, complete with party plans, lists of reliable wine merchants, the schedules of various bards and musicians, cocktail recipes, and occasionally the music and lyrics of good drinking songs. Each Celebrant's portfolio is given its own name by its owner, but often reflects the nature of its contents such as Portfunlio, Portefeuille Amusant, or simply Fun Book. When Celebrants get together, they'll often share information with each other, creating their own libraries from which to draw inspiration.

Once per year in Diegina, on the winter solstice, Celebrants from around Alacar get together in a massive celebration called Adin Noctis, which roughly translates into "loud night," that lasts from dusk until dawn. During this time the Celebrants nominate and vote, by the sound of their applause, for a new Monarch of Fun who leads the congregation for a year. This "monarch" acts as a figurehead for the church, helps determine where more of their representation is needed, and initiates any special projects, such as helping rebuild after a war or throwing a party to celebrate a nation's monarch's new progeny.

Revelry & Restraint

These two siblings (currently most often seen as brothers) represent the struggle of mortals to either relax too much to the point of laziness or keep a joyless regimen of discipline and duty. They travel Alacar together, each trying to convince the other that their way is best, often leaving memorable stories for mortals who cross their paths. Neither is particularly dangerous while they balance each other out, but either taken by themselves can be catastrophic for mortals. Overall, mortals tend to enjoy their time when the two siblings come calling.

Castilli, God of Restraint.name: 卡斯蒂利，克制之神 Castilli, God of Restraint
Castilli, God of Restraint.text:
礼仪侯爵

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.ezz0wctJEKrqKwOV]{Castilli}

阵营： LN（LG、LN、LE）

兄弟姐妹：阿蒙

神殿：学校、官僚机构办公室、图书馆、监狱

崇拜者：立法者、卫兵

圣兽：狗

圣色：黑色与蓝色

领域：自信、守护、真理、暴政

牧师法术：1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.o4lRVTwSxnOOn5vl]{Sleep}，3环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.DCQHaLrYXMI37dvW]{Paralyze}，5环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.GP3wewkQXEPrLxYj]{Subconscious Suggestion}

偏好武器：流星锤索

神圣能力：体质或感知

神圣技能：社交

神圣恩泉：伤害或治疗

关键典范：保持冷静、只做必要之事、将自身风险降到最低

关键禁忌：自愿摄入毒素或药物、破坏社交礼仪、愚蠢行事

对卡斯蒂利而言，举止得体、外表得体、并被以得体方式对待，就是一切。在社交规则方面，卡斯蒂利真的写下了那本书；而且书名就叫《社交互动规则》。标题确实干巴巴的，但直截了当——这正是礼仪侯爵的偏好。他的崇拜者会根据当地习俗与“何为礼貌”的变化来研读并作批注，因此，卡斯蒂利的圣典会因读到的版本不同、来自不同的地区与时代而给出不同的指引。

尽管卡斯蒂利最有影响力、也最广为人知的祭司同时也是贵族阶层的一员，但他们并非统治的贵胄。相反，他们往往是家中的第三或第四个孩子，几乎没有继承土地的机会。他们在上流社会中的职责包括：教导贵族子弟，使其能更好地在同侪间行事；协调加冕等重大社交活动；以及在宫廷中斥责那些越矩之人。在缺乏强势贵族阶层的地区，他们也会以教师、讼律师，甚至偶尔以狱卒的身份出现——不过是专注于改造与复健的那种。

卡斯蒂利的教会会召开地区会议，讨论风格、仪式与习俗等事务。其中规模最大的会议，理所当然地被称为“卡斯蒂拉里乌姆”。在此活动中，出席邻近贵族宫廷的祭司们会聚集起来讨论宫廷阴谋，展示并裁定最新时尚，并且偶尔对故意在宫廷中扰乱礼仪的贵族施以惩罚。最著名的例子之一发生在482年的卡斯蒂拉里乌姆：他们规定每餐前后都要洗手，以防止瘟疫传播——这一习俗延续至今。

尽管卡斯蒂利的批评者可能认为其信仰与个人自由的理念正面相悖，但其神职人员会辩称：卡斯蒂利希望凡人拥有自由，只是要保持文明，并以适当的方式行使自由。在更为混沌的地区——例如靛蓝群岛更荒野的区域——卡斯蒂利的追随者会秘密行事，或以更低调的方式实践信仰；但他们仍会尽可能维持礼仪规则，例如担任海盗纠纷与其他类似冲突的仲裁者。

他的祭司也最有可能为那些长期深陷“过度”之苦的人开设收容所与复健计划。他们常出入酒馆、水烟馆等人们自愿使用致醉物的场所，并对那些过量者施以治疗手段，或为那些即将伤害自己的人提供引导。

卡斯蒂利本人与他的兄弟阿蒙一同周游世界，试图阻止他惹上太多麻烦。人们常在某个聚落里短暂目击到这对兄弟：阿蒙煽动民众，而卡斯蒂利紧随其后，努力控制局势。在阿蒙能制造足够的混沌以分散兄长注意力的时期，卡斯蒂利往往需要把兄弟护理回健康状态——通常还会附上一顿训诫。尽管如此，两人彼此相爱；但这并不妨碍阿蒙无视卡斯蒂利的警告，继续怂恿凡人做出不良行为。

现实世界的含义

尽管阿蒙与卡斯蒂利组成了一对诙谐的搭档，但重要的是必须承认：物质滥用会导致严重问题，绝非笑谈。他们的二元对照、他们对“天平”的贡献，是为了提醒我们：即便享乐，也必须保持责任感。请务必在你自己的生活中找到狂欢与克制的平衡，才能确保自身安全。

Marquis of Civility

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.ezz0wctJEKrqKwOV]{Castilli}

Alignment: LN (LG, LN, LE)

Siblings: Amon

Temples: schools, bureaucratic offices, libraries, prisons

Worshippers: lawmakers, guards

Sacred Animal: dog

Sacred Colors: black and blue

Domains: confidence, protection, truth, tyranny

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.o4lRVTwSxnOOn5vl]{Sleep}, 3rd: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.DCQHaLrYXMI37dvW]{Paralyze}, 5th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.GP3wewkQXEPrLxYj]{Subconscious Suggestion}

Favored Weapon: bola

Divine Ability: Constitution or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Society

Divine Font: harm or heal

Key Edicts: Stay levelheaded, do only what is required, minimize your risk

Key Anathema: Willingly experience a toxin or drug, break social decorum, act foolish

Acting, looking, and being treated appropriately is everything to Castilli. When it comes to the rules of social interaction, Castilli literally wrote the book; it's even actually called The Rules of Social Interaction. A dry title, to be sure, but straight to the point, which is exactly how The Marquis of Civility prefers it. His worshipers study and annotate it, based on local customs and changes in what is considered polite, so Castilli's holy book might give different instruction depending on which version one reads and from which region and era.

While the most influential and widely known priests of Castilli are also members of the aristocracy, they aren't ruling nobles. Instead, they are often a third or fourth child who has little chance of inheriting land. Their functions in polite society include tutoring the children of aristocrats so that they can better function among their peers, coordinating major social events such as coronations, and reprimanding those who step out of line at court. In regions without a strong aristocracy, they can also be found as teachers, barristers, and sometimes even jailors, though ones who focus on rehabilitation.

The church of Castilli holds regional conferences to discuss matters of style, ritual, and custom. The largest of these is, appropriately enough, know as Castillarium. At this event priests who attend the nearby noble courts gather to discuss courtly intrigue, present and decide on the newest fashions, and occasionally administer punishments for nobles who have purposely disrupted decorum in the court. In one of the most famous examples, during the Castillarium of 482 they dictated the washing of hands before and after every meal to prevent the spread of plague, a custom that continues to this day.

Though detractors of Castilli might see his faith as being in direct opposition to the idea of individual freedom, his clergy would argue that Castilli wants mortals to have freedom but to remain civil and practice their freedom in an appropriate way. In more chaotic regions, such as the wilder areas of the Indigo Isles, the adherents of Castilli practice in secret or in a more low key mannet, but they still maintain any rules of decorum they can, such as becoming arbiters of pirate disputes and other similar conflicts.

His priests are also some of the most likely to open shelters and rehabilitation programs for those habitually suffering from excess. They frequent taverns, hookah clubs, and other locales where intoxicants are used willingly, and employ their healing techniques on those who have gone too far, or give guidance to those who are about to harm themselves.

Castilli himself travels the world with his brother, Amon, trying to keep him from getting in too much trouble. The pair are often sighted for brief moments in one settlement or another with Amon riling up the populace and Castilli in his wake trying to keep the situation under control. In times where Amon can cause sufficient chaos to distract his brother, Castilli oft needs to nurse his brother back to good health, usually with the addition of a lecture. Nevertheless, the two love each other, but that doesn't stop Amon from continuing to instigate bad behavior from mortals, despite Castilli's warnings.

Real World Implications

Though Amon and Castilli make a humorous duo, it is important to acknowledge that substance abuse can lead to serious issues and is no laughing matter. Their dichotomy, their contribution to The Balance, is here to remind us that though we may enjoy ourselves we need to stay responsible. Be sure to find the balance of revelry and restraint in your own life so you can remain safe.

Ebrugeses, God of Feasts.name: 埃布鲁格瑟斯，宴飨之神 Ebrugeses, God of Feasts
Ebrugeses, God of Feasts.text:
过度女王

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.eTvWv6VuUhSsyZlr]{Ebrugeses}

阵营: CN（CG、N、CN、CE）

兄弟姐妹: Havath

神殿: 餐馆、酒馆、剧院、农场

崇拜者: 厨师、享乐主义者、农夫、酒馆老板

圣兽: 猪

圣色: 绿色与紫色

领域: 家庭、放纵、月亮、财富

牧师法术: 1环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Wu0xFpewMKRK3HG8]{Grease}，3环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.F9mA2Bg27QKniIdv]{Bottomless Stomach}，4环：@Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.ER1LOEgCLtmEKd05]{Ravenous Portal}

偏好武器: 巨斧

神授能力: 体质或魅力

神授技能: 制作

神授圣能: 治疗

关键典范: 享受你所拥有的；尽情享用；与他人一同分享生命的丰饶

关键禁忌: 剥夺他人的丰饶；适度消费；沉溺于你所没有的东西

盛宴、过度、享乐！这些正是Ebrugeses为之而活的事物：一场永无止境的庆典，美食、佳酿与同伴对所有人都触手可及。只要还有更多乐趣可寻，她就不相信“节制”。尽管有人称她贪食，但那些人并不理解：沉浸于物质之乐也能找到何等的喜悦。再咬一口馅饼，再啜一口美酒，再与朋友笑闹一阵——这便是崇拜过度女王的精髓。

她的崇拜者有酿酒师与厨师，也有音乐家与裁缝。他们喜爱钱能买到的最上等之物，也珍视那些钱买不到的少数珍宝。他们在第一道菜上桌前为她举杯，又在最后一道菜后歌颂她的功绩。Ebrugeses的奉献者并不只是负担得起珍馐与华美丝绸的富人。各行各业的人都能享受生命在身边赐予的丰饶。某些最喧闹、最纵情的Ebrugeses献礼盛宴，反而发生在社区中心或农舍里，人们聚在一起，分享他们共同的财富。

她的大多数庆典都在月光下尽兴而为。信徒们说，是她祝福了月亮，使其牵引海潮。正因如此，渔民才能捕获大量鱼获，让沿海社群得以大快朵颐，并将其出口到邻近之地。也有人说，你能在月面上看见她的微笑，仿佛她正俯照Alacar，向月色皎洁之下的每一个族裔与生物赐予祝福。

Ebrugeses的一些祭司努力为社群中贫困者带来食物、衣物与物资。Cordiam教派的这些成员将自身的神圣职责视为：把女神的财富与丰饶带给Alacar的所有人。Cordite相信，Havath是一名敌人，而能让他仅仅被压制住的，唯有灵体的慷慨与携手共行。他们帮助分配财富，并且常与Ebrugeses祭司团的其他教派相互龃龉。

Avarn教派的成员奉行一句格言：“多即是多。”他们相信，通过积累财富与力量，他们将飞升至Ebrugeses宏伟的宫廷，在永无止境的庆典中度过永恒。他们尽其所能充实自己的钱箱，手段包括狡计、机智、商业头脑，有时甚至是盗窃。Avarnite相信，Havath会带走一部分人而非所有人——他会挑选那些最弱小、手头资源最少者。他们认为，只要比邻居拥有更多，就能避开白骨王子，以及他对一切生命永不满足的饥渴。不必多说，他们与Cordite陷入无休止的仇争；而Havath只是咧嘴一笑，夺走他想要的一切。

Ebrugeses与Amon相处得相当融洽；若不是他那扫兴的兄弟总是跟在身旁，她会经常邀请他参加自己的派对。Castilli与Ebrugeses只要同处一地便会争吵斗殴，给任何欢乐场合都蒙上一层阴影。她憎恨他，甚于所有其他子嗣——但有一人例外：她所鄙弃的兄弟Havath。两者有时同在便会拳脚相向，因此其他子嗣往往会出面干预，尽量让他们彼此保持距离。

盛宴与饥荒

Ebrugeses与Havath最初的神性火花，源自人群之间食物分配的不均：一方拥有足以维生的富余，另一方却连生存所需都不足。这股能量变得如此失衡，以至于开始自发地拥有“生命”。双亲意识到这股力量增长得何等迅猛，明白他们必须介入。他们诞下盛宴与饥荒之子——分别是Ebrugeses与Havath——以夺回对平衡的掌控。

Queen of Excess

@Compendium[battlezoo-world-of-indigo-isles-pf2e.indigo-isles-deities.eTvWv6VuUhSsyZlr]{Ebrugeses}

Alignment: CN (CG, N, CN, CE)

Siblings: Havath

Temples: restaurants, pubs, theaters, farms

Worshippers: chefs, Epicureans, farmers, tavern owners

Sacred Animal: hog

Sacred Colors: green and purple

Domains: family, indulgence, moon, wealth

Cleric Spells: 1st: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.Wu0xFpewMKRK3HG8]{Grease}, 3rd: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.F9mA2Bg27QKniIdv]{Bottomless Stomach}, 4th: @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.ER1LOEgCLtmEKd05]{Ravenous Portal}

Favored Weapon: greataxe

Divine Ability: Constitution or Charisma

Divine Skill: Crafting

Divine Font: heal

Key Edicts: enjoy what you have, consume your fill, partake of life's bounty with others

Key Anathema: deprive others of their own bounty, consume in moderation, dwell on what you don't have

Feasts, excess, enjoyment! These are the things Ebrugeses lives for: a celebration without end where good food, drink, and company are available to all. She doesn't believe in moderation when there is still more fun to be had. Though some have called her gluttonous, those people don't understand the delight that can be found in the enjoyment of material things. Another bite of pie, another sip of wine, and another laugh with friends: this is the essence of what it is to worship the Queen of Excess.

Her worshipers are vintners and chefs, musicians and tailors. They're people who delight in the finest things money can buy and a few things that it can't buy. They raise a glass to her before their first course and sing her praises after the last course. Ebrugeses' devotees aren't just the rich who can afford gourmet cuisine and fine silks. People of all walks of life can enjoy the bounty that life has provided around them. Some of the most uproarious feasts honoring Ebrugeses are in community centers or farmhouses where folk come together to share in their collective wealth.

Most of her celebrations are enjoyed by the light of the moon. Adherants say that she's blessed the moon to bring the ocean's tides. Because of her, fishers can catch large bounties of fish for coastal communities to feast on and export to nearby lands. Still others say that you can see her smile on the moon's surface, like she's beaming down on Alacar, wishing a blessing on every single ancestry and creature under the moon's shining light.

Some of Ebrugeses's priests work to bring food, clothes, and goods to members of their community that have little. These members of the Cordiam Sect see their divine duty as bringing the goddess's wealth and bounty to all people of Alacar. Cordites believe that Havath is a foe only kept at bay through generosity of spirit and working together. They help to share the wealth, and are often at odds with other sects of Ebrugeses's priesthood.

Members of the Avarn Sect live by the adage: \"More is more.\" They believe that by accumulating wealth and power they will ascend to Ebrugeses's grand court and live out eternity in never-ending celebrations. They seek to fill their own coffers as best they can through guile, wit, business acumen, or sometimes even theft. Avarnites believe that Havath will take some and not others, choosing those weakest and with the fewest resources at hand. They believe that by having more than their neighbor they'll avoid the Bone Prince and his unsatiated hunger for all life. Needless to say, they are engaged in an endless feud with Cordites, while Havath simply grins and takes what he wants.
