Surely vast quantities of stolen booty can't be making their way up that deathtrap of a staircase, and there are no sea-level coves or caves that I can see—but it will take braver folk than me to investigate more closely.

The Beacon

Since the construction of the Beacon fifty years ago, there have been far fewer shipwrecks than in days past. I understand that there are some elements in Mistvale that prefer the old days before the lighthouse was completed. But you can't please everyone, especially divers who make a living from sunken treasure.

Another local sore spot regarding the Beacon is that outsiders run it: followers of the Eld who revere a powerful spirit of light. They've shown themselves to be incorruptible in their lighthouse-keeping duties and are also generous with healing and other magical gifts. Many a Mistvale sailor owes their life, or at least a limb, to the Beacon's keepers. Nevertheless, the Eld faith hasn't made much headway here, perhaps because the Beacon's keepers have never expressed a theory or a religious belief about Mistvale's ancient disappearances. Despite their reticence, I think the keepers' presence in Mistvale might have something to do with that old mystery.

Cuarc's Laboratory

Despite the xotlxotl wizard's easy and amiable manner, or perhaps in part because of it, the locals tend to give Cuarc's laboratory a wide berth. That's to my advantage, and a good merchant knows how to sniff out an opportunity. It makes it easier to broker deals and trades, where I lend her the supplies she needs for her experiments in exchange for arcane oddities she's created or discovered. Never in my visits have I witnessed an explosion, out of control summoned beast, spatial distortion, or other stereotypical arcane experimental disaster that might warn the other townsfolk away from the xotlxotl's laboratory, but I can't help but feel like that's the sense I get when I ask the other inhabitants of Mistvale about her.

Third Burrow

Outside the town proper, just on the edge of the Mist and its depths, lies a small communal living space used by some of the Third Timers. From there, it's easier to begin one of their parades into the woods, though other Mistvalers would never live so close.

Nilbog: A Tale of Two Towns.name: 尼尔博格：双城记 Nilbog: A Tale of Two Towns
Nilbog: A Tale of Two Towns.text:
Nilbog

Settlement 5

  • N
  • Town

Mining and smithing town with a nightly dream market.

Government Dual mayorship

Population 3,027 (39% kragraks, 37% bannog, 18% hardriggans, 6% others)

Languages Common, Terran, Aklo, Jotun

Religions the Balance, the Eld

Threats mine monsters, nightmare creatures, thorn thicket denizens

Mining Magnet The best miners and smiths in Bluebell live in Nilbog. As such, characters can purchase metal weapons and armor up to 12th level in Nilbog.

Boss Belk male hardriggan foreman of the iron mine, elected leader during the day

Yonce the Seer nonbinary bannog priest and dreamseeker, elected leader during the night

Arguth female kragrak master weaponsmith

A famous mining and metalworking community a day's travel west from Seaview, Nilbog produces the best steel in all the Indigo Isles. Nilbog knives practically sharpen themselves—and I joke that they sell themselves too! I always pick up a few when I'm in town. No Seaview chef will settle for anything less than a Nilbog blade, especially if they know their competitor already has one.

Besides steel, the Nilbog mines also produce an improbable stream of other stones and metals. Small quantities of silver and gold, garnets and rubies, and other precious minerals emerge from the delve. Miners occasionally excavate more surprising things as well. Pearls, cut gems, and gold coins of unfamiliar stamps have been unearthed in Nilbog, along with stranger finds. Some of its residents believe that the miners have tapped into a deposit of something uncanny, such as a stray vein from the Plane of Earth or the Dimension of Dreams. Some even prophesy that there will be a payment due for the mine's many gifts to the town. As a person of business, I can't afford to turn down today's profit for fear of tomorrow's bill, but nonetheless, I confess that I find Nilbog rather eerie, especially after dark.

The rocky ground at the foot of Nilbog Forest has long been home to a kragrak community, and they've been extracting iron from the ground for who knows how long, certainly before Seaview or Hinter came to be. But the Nilbog of today is different than it was long ago. The staid mining town underwent a metamorphosis a hundred years ago when a population of bannog settled in Nilbog.

The cause for the influx of bannog is a matter of local dispute. When I've visited Nilbog, I've heard a different theory just about every time I've asked the question. Some say that it all started when a kragrak digger emerged from the mine clutching a handful of brightly colored eggs, which hatched into the first of Nilbog's bannog residents. Others claim that one night the townsfolk shared a vivid dream about a village of spooky rabbit people, and when they awoke, they found the dream village superimposed on their own. While the various local stories tend similarly toward the fantastic, in Hinter, to the north, a somewhat more prosaic story is told. According to this account, Hinter Market employed a significant bannog population until the marketgoers began complaining. The tools the bannog vendors sold worked in unusual ways or not at all, and those who ate their foods sometimes experienced strange visions and nightmares. After a time, Hinter residents say, these bannog were kicked out of Hinter and emigrated to Nilbog, where they founded the Dream Market.

Life in Nilbog

Apart from some busy streets in Seaview, nowhere else in Bluebell is there business to be done continuously from one dawn to the next—but nowhere else do I take such care to be inside by sundown. Nilbog seems nothing more than an industrious kragrak mining town by day but transforms into an eerie bannog bazaar by night.

Each day, kragrak delvers work the mines, dumping ore and coal into massive wagons. Laborers haul the ore wagons along tracks to blast furnaces, which operate from sunup to sundown. The smoke from the furnaces can be seen halfway across the island.

Not far from the furnaces, dozens of smiths hammer and tap, turning raw materials into finished goods or turning out metal bars for sale to other crafters. Nilbog smiths produce every kind of metal good, from iron nails to silver cutlery and bronze cooking pots. Nilbog steel is especially valued, and the town's weapons and armorsmiths are justly celebrated throughout the Indigo Isles. For a peddler like me, a trip to the Nilbog smithies is an indispensable part of any island circuit. Two coppers worth of Nilbog nails might sell for a silver in Seaview, Hinter, or Crest.

With nightfall, Nilbog's mining and metalworking cease, and wise businessfolk go to bed. The town's bannog, most of whom have slept through the day, wake up and go about their own business, each carrying a "dream light"—an alchemically treated torch, each of which burns with a different vivid color. This torchlit traffic casts an eerie, multicolored glow over Nilbog, as if hundreds of will-o'-wisps had taken over a town.

At night, many of Nilbog's bannog population perform the same types of tasks that other folks do by day: shopping, paying visits to friends, and even farming. But their most famous activity and Nilbog's most notorious industry, if not its most economically useful, is the nightly dream market.

Under the green, pink, and yellow glow of the dream lights, visitors find dozens of stalls, some exhibiting unfamiliar contraptions, others pies and cakes, and still others offering nothing at all for sale—or at least nothing visible. Some of the Dream Market's wares confer good or bad dreams or even waking hallucinations, while others are minor magical trinkets that help or harm, and many of the items for sale have no apparent use at all. But, as the bannog saying goes, "every dream has its dreamer." Everything in the market, bannog believe, has a potential buyer out there somewhere. And indeed, on most nights, the market finds plenty of customers, as folk flock from all over Bluebell to shop for dreams under its garish lights: the hopeful, the hopeless, thrill seekers, and dilettantes with too much coin for their own good. As a young girl on my first trip to Nilbog, I was once dazzled by the lovely baubles of the Market myself! Certain that I would make a fortune in resale, I spent more than I could afford on toys, gizmos, and carefully packed pastries. The next day, when I opened my pack to sell my treasures, I found little but sludge, shards, and eggshells. The goods of the Dream Market don't travel well beyond Nilbog's borders.

Nilbog by day and night present stark contrasts, but it's not quite a tale of two cities. Strong bonds have developed between the mining and marketing communities. Pairs of miners often work together: a digger, typically a powerful kragrak or hardriggan, and a bannog ore gleaner. Such teams typically collect rich and varied hauls of metals and gemstones and often chance upon other strange items to boot—trinkets destined to become offerings at the Dream Market. Perhaps some bannog unreality extends to the mine itself, allowing it to produce not only the wealth of the earth but treasures drawn from its diggers' dreams. The strategy is not without its perils, as nightmare creatures also sometimes appear in the mines.

People of Nilbog

By day, Nilbog's elected leader is Boss Belk (male hardriggan miner), the no-nonsense foreman of the famed Niblog iron mine. He carefully weighs all the ore extracted by each of Nilbog's miners, ensuring that no one withdraws more than their assigned allotment. Gruff and rude, Boss Belk is also incorruptible and honest to a fault, which normally keeps the mine's dealings safe and stable. Lately, however, his job has been complicated by his tendency to vanish into nothingness for minutes at a time, a condition he says stems from a bad cake he ate one night at the Dream Market. The vanishing episodes worsen when he's angry, so he's been practicing deep breathing and relaxation techniques under the tutelage of his nightly counterpart and de facto leader of the Dream Market, Yonce the Seer (nonbinary bannog priest), in order to help keep himself steady in place here in the waking world.

Yonce, on the other hand, is well-respected by his fellow bannog because he has studied dreams more thoroughly than almost anyone else in Nilbog. Yonce can be found out and about at all hours of the day and night; they claim to neither sleep nor wake anymore but to live continuously in a state between the two, a state that makes them the object of envy and interest to twinned bannog. Yonce is the mayor of the Dream Market and the city's administrator during its night hours. If you have trouble with one of your purchases at the Dream Market, see Yonce. They rigidly adhere to the market's "no returns" policy, but they'll do their best to repair any injury, poisoning, mind-shattering horror, petrification, or hauntings incurred by purchases.

Many people come to Nilbog looking for weapons or armor, and the wisest of these travelers seek out Arguth (female kragrak smith). Nilbog's master weaponsmith can create and improve magic weapons and armor. However, you must provide her with the monster parts and other esoteric ingredients she needs to ply her craft.

Exploring Nilbog

The following is a sample of some of the prominent locations found in Nilbog.

Downtown

Most of Nilbog's bannog population lives in the area known as "downtown," which is essentially a communal warren in Nilbog Woods. An area where hundreds of bannog wake and dream can be an unsettling place to visit: moods of euphoria and terror have settled into its tunnels like stains, and the bannog use these threads of emotion, along with the pallid glows of dream lights, to navigate the maze. Once, when I was forced to squeeze through the tunnels in search of some permit office or other, I was given these directions: "Follow the green lights and take a left down the passage that reminds you of being unprepared for a school examination." As you can expect, that was quite a disaster. Next time I need a permit, that clerk will have to come to me!

Dream Market

The Dream Market is held under overhanging trees at the edge of Nilbog Woods, a few miles from the bustle of the mines and blast furnaces. Each morning, the market is packed up, leaving not a stick behind. Here, a browsing customer can find every manner of oddity with unique and unpredictable effects. Unwary sorts sometimes regret their purchases the next day!

Goods In The Dream Market

In the Dream Market, none of the items for sale are familiar in the slightest. Even the bannog merchants who sell them don't always know what they do. The Dream Market has one hard and fast rule: no refunds. GMs can use the following list as inspiration for items for sale, typically at prices ranging from 1 to 20 gold. Once a creature has made a purchase, bannogs won't sell them a second such item on the same night. These items often (but not always) revert to useless rubbish when taken from Nilbog.

Dumpty Device. An eggbeater that turns with dreamlike slowness. If used on cracked eggs or prepared egg products, it beats them back into unbroken eggs.

Memento Meal. A magic still-life painting of a bowl overflowing with fruit; the fruit can be removed from the picture and eaten, providing a creature with sustenance for one day and revealing the concave image of a skull at the bottom of the painted bowl.

Truth Bubble. An unusually resilient soap bubble (1 hp, hardness 2). If a creature pops the bubble, in their next dream, they will become an invisible, floating bubble able to see actual events chosen by the GM.

Floating Friend. A glowing balloon (1 hp) that casts light like a torch. Its purchaser can order it to follow at a specified distance at a maximum speed of 50 feet or to wait in place, although sometimes while waiting, it gets lonely and tries to find its purchaser.

Bad Egg. A bright, multicolored egg. If purchased, when broken or at an inconvenient time, it hatches into a fully grown jinkin gremlin.

Tomorrow Flower. A flower covered with puffy white seeds. If a creature blows away its seeds, its next dream will be a nightmare in which gigantic puffballs descend on their hometown, poisoning the inhabitants.

Last Bite. A small, dusty cake. If a creature eats the cake, they experience the last moments of a random creature murdered in the past year (in Nilbog or elsewhere), potentially gaining a vital clue to a crime somewhere in the world.

Filucrom. A slide whistle that, whenever played, causes its user to imagine themselves surrounded by a crowd of anthropomorphic fish people, talking and singing in discordant voices. It becomes difficult for them to concentrate, requiring a DC 15 flat check to use actions with the concentrate trait, or the action is lost. This is a mental effect that lasts for 1 minute.

Fairy Dew. An acorn cap full of invisible liquid. If a creature drinks it, their next dream will be an achingly beautiful story of a fairy searching for their lost love

Just in Time Charm. Whether by subtle mental compulsions, manipulating luck, or other means, no one is sure, but the possessor of this necklace tends to show up to meetings on time.

Golden Slumber

Near the Dream Market is an inn, the Golden Slumber, which boasts several dozen comfortable beds and an adjoining campground for less well-to-do visitors to the Dream Market (such as myself). Most travelers stay only a day or two at the Golden Slumber, but it hosts a few long-term residents who return to the Market night after night, unable to tear themselves away from the visions it offers.

Nilbog Mine

Three separate mine entrances lead into the Nilbog mine works. The top levels, which predate the bannog settlement, are orderly, while the lower levels are meandering and unsystematic. The proceeds of whatever a miner draws from the ground, although they must pay a small fee to help maintain the mine for every pound of ore (or whatever else) they extract. Outsiders have just as much right as anyone else to work the mines, so you could make a fortune if you fancy a bit of labor. Outsiders are also called in whenever workers encounter monsters in the mine. Ferrous creatures, such as rust monsters, iron ferns, and ferropaceans, are common. Although it's said that there are worse horrors born from vivid bannog nightmares.

Nilbog Woods

Only a few miles square, Nilbog Woods seems to have more winding paths and trails than should fit inside. The bannogs' warren, Downtown, is only a few hundred yards into the forest, and few venture deeper than that. I've heard it's extraordinarily easy to get lost in the Nilbog Woods; would-be explorers find themselves inexplicably back where they started or on the other side of the forest altogether. Those who persevere and reach the woods' center find a vast, nearly impenetrable thorn thicket. The bannog who live in Nilbog Forest never speak of the thicket or what's inside.

Weapons in Stone

The simple name "Weapons in Stone" graces Arguth's smithy. There, the city's finest weapon and armorsmith plies her trade and sells her wares. When I first entered the building, I had quite a shock, as a ferocious beast seemed to be looming over me. It turned out to be a delivery, freshly slain, from an adventurer seeking to commission a powerful new weapon from the parts.

Goldcrop Isle.name: 金穗岛 Goldcrop Isle
Goldcrop Isle.text:
Marrekunogriz 对最大靛蓝岛屿的内幕消息。

啊哈，冒险者！无论你来到靛蓝群岛是为了追求名声、财富，还是个人的圆满，选择拿起我的指南就说明你走在了正确的道路上。别理会那些唱反调的人——他们声称探索这些岛屿是件蠢事，或试图告诉你波比·冯·藤壶已经把这里的一切都做完了——作为一条丑角巨龙，只有我才有资格宣布谁是蠢货、谁不是蠢货！

啊，不过首先，请允许我自我介绍。我是 Marrekunogriz，但朋友们叫我 Glimmer，因为我敏锐的心智能在他人那遥远的未来里、仍只是潜能的一丝微光之时，就洞见其中的伟大。你也可以叫我 Glimmer；毕竟，等你征服了我挚爱的群岛之谜时，我们就会成为很好的朋友。

金穗岛是构成靛蓝群岛的主要岛屿中最大、也最显眼的一座——不过你最好别让风铃花岛的居民听见你这么说——而且你很可能会在这里首次登陆。海妖港那如同一只舒展的翼、迎接船只驶入的外观，会给人留下耀眼的第一印象。若你选择跳过我们的商业中心，直接前往朗姆木板，你会发现一座充满友善、自称“海盗”的城市，他们总是在庆祝某个节日或另一个节日。g'mayun 们更在意派对，至于海盗行当里劫掠的那部分——基本不在意，甚至完全不在意。

但如果我们实话实说，亲爱的冒险者，你要找的璞玉并不在城市里。任何人都能从富裕王室脚边捡些残羹冷炙、在拥挤的市集里兜售货物，或登上一艘补给充足的船扬帆远航。金穗岛由多种环境构成，从严酷的沙漠到繁茂的雨林，从被淹没的沼泽到高耸的群山——几乎都未被那些自以为征服了一切的城市居民探索过。踏入连商人都不敢涉足之处，寻找那些超出被娇惯的海盗所能触及的宝藏——如此，你便能与波比并肩，在历史中占据一席之地！

Marrekunogriz's inside scoop on the largest Indigo Isle.

Ahoy, adventurer! Whether you come to the Indigo Isles seeking fame, fortune, or personal fulfillment, you've set yourself on the right track by picking up my guide. Ignore the naysayers who claim that exploring the islands is a fool's errand or try to tell you that Poppy von Barnacle has done it all here already—as a harlequin dragon, only I get to declare who is and isn't a fool!

Ah, but first, allow me to introduce myself. I am Marrekunogriz, but friends call me Glimmer because my sharp mind perceives greatness in people when it is but a glimmer of potential in their distant future. You can call me Glimmer; after all, we shall be great friends by the time you've conquered the mysteries of my beloved Isles.

Goldcrop is the largest and most prominent of the main islands that comprise the Indigo Isles, though you shouldn't let Bluebell residents hear you say it, and it is likely to be where you first land. Kraken's Harbor, with its appearance of a sweeping wing welcoming ships in, makes a dazzling impression. Should you choose to skip our center of commerce and proceed to Rumplank, you'll find a city filled with friendly, self-proclaimed "pirates" who are constantly celebrating one festival or another. The g'mayuns are more about the partying and less—if at all—about the pillaging part of piracy.

But if we're being honest, dear adventurer, the cities are not where you will find the diamonds in the rough. Anyone can collect scraps from the feet of a wealthy monarchy, sell their wares in a crowded market, or sail aboard a well-provisioned ship. Goldcrop consists of many environments, from harsh deserts to lush rainforests, flooded marshes to soaring mountains, almost all unexplored by the city dwellers who think they've conquered it all. Tread where merchants dare not, search for treasures out of reach of softened pirates, and that is how you shall take your place in history alongside Poppy!

A Brief History.name: 简史 A Brief History
A Brief History.text:
谜团笼罩着靛蓝岛屿的历史。留存下来的历史记录寥寥无几，而同样站得住脚却相互矛盾的叙述比比皆是。这种情况也延伸到了各个岛屿本身的名字上。大多数人相信“金穗”之名源自“金色山脉”，因为它们的色泽确实金黄，由此引出了关于山中遍布黄金的传闻。传奇说，谁若能找到那些神话般的矿脉，便会发现黄金多到可以像农夫收割小麦一样收获。许多勘探者在山中破产，而他们无一人能挖出愚人金，更别提真正的黄金。

对这个名字还有一种不那么流行、却更可信的解释：这座岛过去远比现在肥沃，火山土壤对庄稼而言宛如黄金，能让万物丰产。如今，只要看看裂喙盐荒漠，就知道这种说法放到现在已站不住脚。克拉肯周边的农场还算足够高产，雾磨与朗普兰克在好年景里也能自给自足，但产量并不值得夸耀。即便如此，许多岛民仍保留着早于我们书面记录之前的口述历史。他们谈起从山中携带沉积物、流得如金一般的河流，以及皮肤上点缀着鎏金斑点的巨人怪物。我也听过关于一种金色植物的故事——有人说它会开出金色花朵，白天储存阳光、夜晚将其释放；也有人说它的茎秆呈金色，可用于一种炼金配方，将贱金属嬗变为贵金属。

总而言之，金穗随时代而变，并仍在持续变化。战栗山是一座活火山，它的震颤重塑着大地——这也是其名字的一个可能来源；也有人说是波比如此命名了这座山。这些反复的剧变埋葬了许多事物，使岛上的诸多秘密被泥土或繁茂的丛林枝叶所覆盖。然而，通过传奇与占卜仍能找到线索。把线索串联起来，你或许仍能在金穗上打击到某种形态的“黄金”。

Mystery shrouds the history of the Indigo Isles. Few historical records remain, and contradictory accounts of equal validity abound. This situation extends to the names of the islands themselves. Most believe the name Goldcrop comes from the Golden Mountains, as they are truly golden in color, which has led to rumors that they are filled with gold. Legends say that whoever locates those mythical veins will find gold so plentiful that they can harvest it like a farmer harvests wheat. Plenty of prospectors have gone bankrupt in the mountains, and none of them managed to turn up fool's gold, much less real gold.

A less popular but more believable explanation for the name is that the island used to be much more fertile and that the volcanic dirt was like gold for crops, growing anything in abundance. Today, one needs to look no further than the Crackbeak Salt Desert to know that such a claim doesn't hold up these days. The farms around Kraken are productive enough, and Molmist and Rumplank can sustain themselves during good years as well, but the yields are nothing to boast about. Still, many islanders retain oral histories from times before our written records. They speak of rivers running gold with sediment from the mountains and giant monsters with gilt-flecked skin. I've also heard tales of a golden plant—some say it grows gold flowers that store sunlight during the day and emit it at night, while others say the stalks are golden and can be used in an alchemical recipe that transmutes base metals into precious ones.

Suffice it to say, Goldcrop has changed over time and continues to do so. The Shivering Mountain is an active volcano whose tremors reshape the land— again, one possible origin for its name, while others say Poppy designated the mountain as such. These repeated upheavals have buried much, with many of the island's secrets covered under dirt or lush jungle foliage. Hints remain, though, through legends and divination. Connect the dots, and you may yet strike some form of gold on Goldcrop.

Geography.name: 地理 Geography
Geography.text:
金穗地拥有复杂的生态系统与众多生物群系，有时甚至在同一个有名称的地区之内也并存多种环境，但为了便于导航，我们可以将其地理简化为六个主要分区。

群山是这座岛屿的核心。最高的山峰高耸入云，终年积雪，雪线之下是一片繁茂的高山森林，也是数个硬脊人（hardriggans）与克拉格拉克（kragraks）社群的家园。黄金群山除“溺没丛林”外，直接影响岛上的所有其他地区。岛屿北部的翡翠丘陵与日出海滩构成了迎风地带。此地区没有主要河流贯穿，但由于携带海洋水汽的信风撞上黄金群山而产生充沛降雨。雨水支撑了茂盛的植被，但并不会形成像金穗地其他部分那样会让导航变得困难的密林。

在群山的另一侧是背风地带。群山阻挡了降雨，但事情并不止于此。鉴于裂喙盐沙漠突兀地与繁茂的丛林相邻，这片沙漠呈现出曾经处于完全不同气候的迹象。然而，背风位置确实直接导致了俄卡皮丘陵的干旱特性。根据一种理论，过去来自群山的河流曾为此地的湖泊供水，但地震活动切断了融雪径流。不过，这仍无法解释裂喙盐沙漠与溺没丛林之间如此鲜明的分界。

两处主要的河流补给分区随之形成：南方的迷雾河与东方的触手河。这两条河从群山发源，带来淡水与养分，维系着坐落于各自河口的主要城市——朗木板城（Rumplank）与海妖城（Kraken）。

在这些分区之间，是东南部的沼泽地：翻搅沼泽与战栗丛林。这些低洼区域遭受来自靛蓝海的咸水倒灌。越深入内陆，能找到的淡水生物越多；而海岸则红树林密布，几乎已成为海的一部分。

最后，西北部的溺没丛林是一片雨林，其气候与金穗地的其余部分相互隔绝。一些学者认为是魔法驱动了其永不停歇的降雨。无论真假，这片丛林充满了其他任何地方都不存在的独特动植物。其居民严密守护着在该区域中如何导航的秘密，使之对外来者而言成为死亡陷阱。

Goldcrop has a complex ecosystem with many biomes, sometimes within a single named region, but we can simplify the geography down to six major zones for ease of navigation.

The mountains are the heart of the island. The tallest peaks extend so high that snow covers them year-round, with a healthy alpine forest lying beneath the snowline and home to several communities of hardriggans and kragraks. The Golden Mountains directly influence all the other regions of the island except the Drowned Jungle. The Emerald Hills and Sunrise Beach in the island's northern section comprise the windwards. No major rivers flow through this region, but it sees healthy rainfall as trade winds carrying moisture from the ocean run up against the Golden Mountains. The rain supports lush vegetation but not the dense forests that make navigation difficult in other parts of Goldcrop.

On the opposite side of the mountains are the leewards. The mountains block rainfall, but that's not the whole story. Given that the Crackbeak Salt Desert jarringly borders lush jungles, the desert has signs of once being a completely different climate. However, the leeward location directly leads to the arid nature of the Okapi Hills. According to one theory, rivers from the mountains used to feed lakes in this area, but seismic activity cut off the snowmelt flow. However, that still doesn't explain the sharp cutoff between the Crackbeak Salt Desert and the Drowned Jungle.

Two major river-fed zones arise: the Misty River to the south and the Tentacle River to the east. These rivers flow from the mountains, bringing fresh water and nutrients to sustain the major cities—Rumplank and Kraken, respectively—that sit at their mouths.

Between those zones lie the southeast swamplands of the Writhing Swamp and the Shivering Jungle, low-lying areas that suffer saltwater intrusion from the Indigo Sea. The further inland you go, the more freshwater creatures you find, while the coasts are thick with mangroves and almost part of the sea itself.

Finally, the Drowned Jungle in the northwest is a rainforest whose climate is isolated from the rest of Goldcrop. Some scholars believe that magic drives its perpetual rainfall. Whether true or not, the jungle is full of unique flora and fauna that don't live anywhere else. Its inhabitants closely guard the secrets of how to navigate the area, making it a death trap for outsiders.

Crackbeak Salt Desert and Okapi Hills.name: 裂喙盐沙漠与㺢㹢狓丘陵 Crackbeak Salt Desert and Okapi Hills
Crackbeak Salt Desert and Okapi Hills.text:
很少有人会心甘情愿地踏入裂喙盐漠。此地没有已知的聚居地；就算真有，也肯定不会与兰木板城或丛林彼端的沫雾村维持往来。外来者愿意来此，往往不是为了寻觅宝藏，就是为了逃离一段比这片严酷土地更危险的过去。有大量地质证据表明，裂喙曾是湖泊与溪流遍布、繁荣兴盛的区域，但无人知晓裂喙为何会干涸。许多学者认为，毗邻的溺没丛林以魔法手段维持其生态系统，而裂喙在地震切断来自黄金山脉的水流后，便逐渐干涸。另一方面，对立（而且更有意思！）的理论认为：某种危险的魔法——也许是昔日居住于此的吉里加提文明所释放——导致了干旱；而溺没丛林的气候反倒更接近此地的自然常态。两派都无法证明各自理论，因此双方学者常常派遣冒险者，借由某些离奇的实验来试图证实自己的主张。

当然，探索沙漠说起来容易做起来难！危险的盐风暴与刺眼的烈日，只是困扰此地最显眼的两种威胁。钻掘虫与食肉掘行者自下方潜伏追踪猎物；同时它们对生态系统也至关重要，因为它们的行动会创造地下隧道，而这些隧道也兼作水库。经验老到的生存专家知道要寻找水草——其根系伸入这些水库，并在鳞茎中储存净化后的水——以及如何将其与外形相近的手枪草区分开来；后者会攻击并吞食活生物。

霍羚丘陵构成兰木板城周边较湿润区域与沙漠本体之间的边界。雨水充沛的冬季里，山丘覆满绿草；其余时节则干枯成金黄草秆，供养部分野生动物与驯养的提亚拉冬。相对缺乏掩蔽或高营养植物，使得此地几乎没有值得一提的掠食者，因此成为城里人热衷的去处：他们想找一片荒野来检验自己的毅力，却又不愿承担过多艰辛。丘陵最初得名的霍羚如今已不在此地生活；它们的食性需要大量森林掩蔽，随着气候变干，它们迁入了迷雾丛林。

人群与文化

被称为迁徙者的游牧g'mayun在北方丘陵与颤寒山脚下的迷雾丛林之间往返。他们崇拜“平衡”四位神祇，以万神殿形式象征四季——卡萨、奇拉、恰基萨与韦拉。他们的生活方式随季节更迭而变化，映照诸神的侧重：夏季围绕施舍与分享丰收；秋季专注于为将至的寒冷采集补给；冬季引向静默的沉思与为来年做准备；春季则播种并滋养土地。他们在旅行途中也会照料大地，确保万物平衡，使动植物皆能繁盛。他们相信，这种生活方式是对春之神祇奇拉的最佳致敬——正是奇拉将g'mayun从恶魔的影响中拯救出来。

焦灼者是生活在裂喙的盐与沙克拉格拉克组成的游牧社会。他们奉行孤立主义，遵循其创始者雷库卡尔的教诲；雷库卡尔认为，让孩子接触其他文化会稀释自身文化。他们沉浸于自身环境，专注保存祖先的根源。多数旅人不会与他们相遇，因为他们尽力避开外来者；但若有人陷入危难，他们也很少会出手相救。刻意尝试与他们接触通常只会遭到礼貌拒绝；而对于无视他们抽身意图的人，他们往往会转为敌对。

关注地点

传言称，沙漠中心某处存在一座吉里加提金库，内藏令人难以置信的魔法发明配方。吉里加提是由多种族裔构成的半游牧发明者群体，在此地仍郁郁葱葱时曾生活于此。随着裂喙干涸，他们分散至整座岛屿，而他们的大部分成果也湮没于时代之中。尽管他们的发明已是数百年前的旧物，其工艺之精密却使得其中一些至今仍无人能及。重新发现他们的配方，带来的收获不仅属于历史学家，更将惠及整个社会。

要定位这座理论上的金库绝非易事。一些矿业公司在黄金山脉淘金失望而归后，曾尝试投身此事。他们假设任何金库都需要大量金属来建造，便用探矿术去侦测高浓度矿石。然而沙漠辽阔无边，使得他们的成功率比大海捞针还要渺茫。

若这些探矿者肯动动心智，就会意识到：要进入这座神话般的金库，他们需要钥匙。吉里加提历史的学者会第一个告诉你：由于他们四处漂泊，他们发明了兼具安全装置的导航工具。他们偏爱一种能投影“从故乡地点所见星辰位置”的星盘。通过将当前位置测得的星辰数据与投影位置对照，吉里加提便能从任何地方找到回家的路。这些星盘也兼作钥匙：每一只都有独特的凹槽，用以插入故乡某处特定的锁中。能开启金库的那只星盘，或许正蒙尘于某位吉里加提后裔的家中，被当作精致的镇纸使用，却未意识到它足以开启数不尽的财富！

若想找个更放松的尝试，不妨在霍羚丘陵的养生静修地欢歌停留；其主人是g'mayun励志演说家Lacha'ala Nim（性别流动的g'mayun励志演说家）。它自称是丘陵里唯一的永久建筑——当然，不算那些帐篷几乎等同永久的各式营地。

不管是谁发明了那句“傻子和他们的金子很容易分开”，多半是在欢歌这里顿悟的。我并不是说拉恰'阿拉有任何恶意；我从未见过比他更真诚的人，但傻子往往也很真诚，因为他们不懂。这里你可以买到“日吻之水”，每瓶1 sp——这是暗号，指把冰水放在太阳下刚好放到冰块融化；不过他们声称其中注入了焕新的光能量——以及其他惊人的“优惠”，比如：5 sp一份腌水果，1 gp一套填充了枯死霍羚草的寝具，或15 gp让你被锁在汗蒸帐篷里一小时。不过，那些翼部按摩确实值回所有金子——千万别错过！如果你没有翅膀，那我只能遗憾地说：你真的错过太多了！

Few willingly wander into the Crackbeak Salt Desert. No known settlements exist in this area, and if any do, they certainly don't maintain relationships with the city of Rumplank or the village of Molmist past the jungle. Outsiders only willingly come here seeking treasure or fleeing a past more dangerous than the harsh land. There is significant geological evidence that Crackbeak used to be a thriving region full of lakes and streams, but no one knows why Crackbeak dried out. Many scholars believe that the adjacent Drowned Jungle sustains its ecosystem via magical means and that Crackbeak slowly dried up after earthquakes cut off water flow from the Golden Mountains. On the other hand, the rival (and much more interesting!) theory is that dangerous magic, perhaps unleashed by the Jirigati civilization that used to live in the region, caused the aridity, and the Drowned Jungle's climate is closer to the natural order in the area. Neither side can prove their theory, so both sets of scholars often send adventurers to try to prove their claims with some bizarre experiment.

Of course, exploring the desert is easier said than done! Dangerous salt storms and the glaring sun are just two of the most visible dangers that plague the area. Drillworms and carnivorous burrowers stalk their prey from below while being critical to the ecosystem as their activity creates underground tunnels that double as water reservoirs. Keen survivalists know to look for waterweed, plants whose roots extend into these reservoirs and store purified water in their bulbs, and how to distinguish them from the similar-looking pistolweed, which instead attacks and devours living creatures.

The Okapi Hills constitute the border between the wetter region around Rumplank and the desert itself. Green grass covers the hills during the wet winters and dries into golden stalks the rest of the year, supporting some wildlife and domesticated thyaladons. The relative lack of cover or nutritional plants means this region lacks predators of note, making it a popular destination for city folk seeking a wilderness to test their grit without risking significant hardship. The okapi for whom the hills were originally named, no longer live in this area as their diet requires significant forest cover, and they migrated into the Misty Jungle as the climate dried out.

People and Culture

Nomadic g'mayun called the Migration move between the northern hills and the Misty Jungle at the base of the Shivering Mountain. They worship the Balance's four gods, representing the four seasons as a pantheon—Kasa, Chira, Chakisa, and Wera. Their way of life changes each season, reflecting the gods' priorities. Summer revolves around charity and sharing bounties; autumn is focused on gathering supplies for the cold ahead; winter leads to quiet contemplation and preparation for the year ahead; and they plant and nurture the land during the spring. They also tend to the earth as they travel, ensuring everything is balanced so that flora and fauna alike can thrive. This lifestyle, they believe, is the best way to honor Chira, deity of spring, for saving g'mayuns from demonic influence.

The Scorched are a nomadic society of salt and sand kragraks who live in Crackbeak. They are isolationists, following the teachings of their founder, Rekukarr, who believes that exposing their children to other cultures dilutes their own. They immerse themselves in their environment and focus on preserving ancestral traditions. Most travelers won't encounter them as they try to avoid outsiders, but they rarely intervene to save someone in distress. Deliberate attempts to contact them meet polite refusal, and they typically become hostile toward those who ignore their attempts to disengage.

Points of Interest

Rumors persist of a Jirigati vault somewhere in the desert's center containing formulas for incredible magical inventions. The Jirigati were semi-nomadic inventors of many ancestries who lived in the area when it was still lush. They dispersed across the island as Crackbeak dried up, and much of their work became lost to time. Though their inventions are now hundreds of years old, the intricacy of their work means some remain unsurpassed to this day. Rediscovering their formulas would be a bounty not just for historians but for society in general.

Locating this theoretical vault would be no simple matter. Some mining companies, fresh off the disappointment of prospecting for gold in the Golden Mountains, have attempted the venture. They assumed that any vault would require a significant amount of metal to construct and used their dowsing techniques to detect large concentrations of ore. However, the desert is immense, which made their odds much worse than finding a needle in a haystack.

Had the prospectors used their minds, they would've realized they needed a key to enter this mythical vault. Scholars of Jirigati history will be the first to tell you that since they wandered so much, they invented navigational tools that doubled as security devices. They favored astrolabes that projected the position of stars as seen from a home location. By comparing the measurements of the stars at their current location to their projected positions, Jirigati could find their way home from anywhere. These astrolabes doubled as keys, each with unique grooves to fit into a specific lock at home. The astrolabe that unlocks the vault might be gathering dust in some Jirigati descendant's home, being used as a fancy paperweight without realizing its potential to unlock countless riches!

For a more relaxing endeavor, consider stopping at the Okapi Hills wellness retreat named Jubilant Song, owned by g'mayun motivational speaker Lacha'ala Nim (genderfluid g'mayun motivational speaker). It claims to be the only permanent structure in the hills, discounting the various campsites whose tents may as well be permanent.

Whoever coined the phrase, "Fools and their gold are easily parted," probably came to the epiphany at Jubilant Song. Now I am not claiming that Lacha'ala is in any way malicious; I have never met a more earnest person, but fools are often earnest because they don't know better. Here you can buy sun-kissed water for 1 sp a bottle— that's code for iced water that's been left out in the sun just long enough for the cubes to melt, though the claim is that it is infused with rejuvenating light energy—and other incredible deals such as 5 sp for pickled fruit, 1 gp for bedding stuffed with dead Okapi grass, and 15 gp to be locked in a sweat tent for an hour. The wing massages are worth all the gold in the world, though; don't miss out on those! If you don't have wings, I'm sorry; you are truly missing out!

Drowned Jungle.name: 溺没丛林 Drowned Jungle
Drowned Jungle.text:
在金茂岛（Goldcrop）上几乎到处都有一个或多个团体试图驯服荒野：从在山中挖掘的勘探者，到误入歧途的远光镇（Farlight）创立者企图征服翡翠丘陵（Emerald Hills）。最后的边境仍是溺没丛林（Drowned Jungle）——一片令人生畏而浓密的雨林，常遭频繁而猛烈的暴雨侵袭，并充斥着危险的生物。

有人说，肆虐此地、也赋予其名号的雷暴并非完全出自自然。溺没丛林与翡翠丘陵之间存在一道难以用地理解释清楚的明确分界；具体来说，就是丛林结束、裂喙沙漠（Crackbeak Desert）开始的那条严酷界线。真相……嗯，众说纷纭。许多地质学家认为溺没丛林的降雨完全自然，而裂喙盐沙漠则受魔法影响——但那套理论太无聊了。来聊聊魔法的解释吧！

居住于此地的盖尔特扎戈里（galtzagorri）谈起“雨之鬼火”（rain wisps）：一种细小的蓝色光点，在倾盆大雨来临前会在区域里飞掠穿梭，其他族裔可能会误认为它们是小妖精（特征）。它们究竟是不是鬼火、甚至是否活着都无人知晓；但据说它们出现时会伴随静电电击的噼啪声，让人浑身汗毛直立。也许是它们带来雨水，又或许它们只是引发风暴的那种所谓魔法所产生的副作用。

一些“制天气者”（Weathermakers；通法（Thongfa）——空气之神祇——的神职人员）宣称：火之神祇宝焰（Baoyan），亦是通法的手足，用魔法把裂喙干燥化以囚禁通法。宝焰的力量造成的气候改变了大气，把干燥的空气困在沙漠上空的热穹之中。随后，宝焰又利用人们对雨水的祈祷引诱通法来到此地，并将她困在那里。她向解救她的冒险者赐予恩惠，而他们请求一处绿洲，以免人们因沙漠而失去家园。虽然她无法拯救裂喙免于其命运，她却创造了溺没丛林作为替代的避难所。这个宗教理论是唯一一个宣称沙漠与丛林两者都具魔法成因、而非只有其一的说法，因此它是我最喜欢的。神祇的作为总比什么“地质力量”之类精彩多了。

另一个故事则围绕凡人试图违逆命运，或伸手攫取超过其应得之物。吉里加提（Jirigati）的后裔低声传说，有一位名为乔尔佩卡（Cholpekka）的哈德里根（hardriggan）发明家（男性哈德里根发明家）企图驾驭“Eld”，为裂喙带来降雨。他在岛屿上四处搜寻水元素强盛之地，并试图说服那里的艾达魔（eldamon）迁往沙漠，把它们的活力一并带去。然而艾达魔无能为力，因为它们追随世界的变化，而非直接影响变化。

在艾达魔一事失败后，乔尔佩卡决定亲自化身水元素，直接成为他所追求的改变。他设计了一场仪式：淹死自己凡躯，并以水之艾达魔的身份复活其灵体。仪式在溺没丛林的核心地带进行——那里水元素强盛——仪式确实将他变形，却也让他受制于同样的限制，把他永远困在丛林之中。

在这一版本里，他仍对丛林有强大的影响：雨水是他的泪水，雷鸣则是他对失败的怒火。此故事的其他变体则把沙漠的成因也归咎于乔尔佩卡，把它当作一则教训：应当满足于自己所拥有的，而非不断渴求更多。在这些传说里，乔尔佩卡寻求力量，想为这片依旧繁荣的地区带来比以往更强效的兴盛；但他失败了，并惹怒了水之艾达魔。它们成群结队地离开该地区，最终导致裂喙盐沙漠的形成。

人群与文化

丛林中唯一的喘息之地是普利提科里村（Pliticori Village）：一处隐秘的盖尔特扎戈里聚落，由无双红宝石（Peerless Ruby）——一头朱红巨龙（雌性朱红巨龙说书人）——创建并治理。村子对他们认为值得信任的人很友好，但丛林的危险使他们不愿与随便什么人打交道。只有被他们视为可靠的人，他们才会主动接触并邀请进入村子。无双红宝石在清除她认为对村子构成威胁者时毫不犹豫；但任何喜欢好故事、也愿意讲些自己的故事（不管是否添油加醋）的冒险者，都会发现她是个热情好客的主人。

巴库（Bacoo）也生活在丛林里，但它们并非典型的友善捣蛋鬼。这些反社交的无赖在厌倦文明后退入丛林，潜行其间，并在精神与肉体层面都把所有生物当作猎物。旅人若被这些巴库压制，仍可通过承诺为它们带来更大的猎物、供它们折磨，来换取活命。然而，这种宽限只会持续到旅人不再有用、或无法继续为巴库奉上邪恶的乐趣为止。在此期间，巴库会不断骚扰受害者，嘲弄他们、对他们施以残酷恶作剧，直到玩腻这些把戏才会将其击倒。

兴趣点

传闻中乔尔佩卡的休息之所位于统称为旱沼（Draught）的一片沼泽之中，坐落在溺没丛林的北部边缘。该区域是巴库的据点，因此验证吉里加提神话真伪是一项危险的尝试。不过，若传闻属实，安抚乔尔佩卡或许能改变溺没丛林的生态系统，使其更安全、更适合探索。

然而，旱沼并非丛林中最危险的地方。那项称号属于石化棕榈（Petrified Palm）：它位于雷娅山（Mount Rhea）的阴影之下，回声山（Mount Echo）以北。这株棕榈是一棵高耸、巨大的棕榈树，不知为何被变形为页岩岩石。它散发死灵能量，将附近的生物腐化成覆着页岩硬壳的不死奴仆。整套由页岩真菌、鸟类、昆虫与哺乳动物组成的生态系统栖居在树上与树内。这些页岩生物极具攻击性；若非受到魔法之雨的遏制——那雨似乎会伤害它们，并迫使它们躲到石化棕榈的叶下——它们很可能把腐化扩散出去。因此，棕榈的居民被丛林本身所制衡，但腐化的威胁始终存在。

最后，若你穿过丛林抵达海岸，你可能会遭遇莫尔米斯特（Molmist）村。我本也能长篇大论地描写莫尔米斯特，但有人说我对镇民的描述过于“鲜艳”。这不是我的错——是人们受不了批评；而因为指出他们根源与规范中的不一致就被赶出城，也不利于对该地点进行深入的介绍。因此，我把你交给当地探险者的能干之手，让他们来描述这座城镇。

One or more groups have attempted to tame the wilderness almost everywhere on Goldcrop, from prospectors digging in the mountains to the misguided founder of Farlight trying to conquer the Emerald Hills. The last frontier remains the Drowned Jungle, a forbidding and dense rainforest subject to frequent, intense downpours and filled with dangerous creatures.

Some say that the thunderstorms that wrack the region and give it its name are not entirely natural in origin. There is a distinct boundary that is not entirely explainable by geography between the Drowned Jungle and Emerald Hills. Specifically, the harsh line where the jungle ends and the Crackbeak Desert begins. The truth… well, there are conflicting tales. Many geologists believe that the Drowned Jungle's rainfall is perfectly natural and that the Crackbeak salt desert is influenced by magic, but that's the boring theory. Let's talk about the magical explanations!

Galtzagorri, who live in the region, speak of rain wisps, tiny blue motes of light that other ancestries might mistake for sprites, that flitter through areas before a torrential downpour begins. Whether they are wisps or even alive is unknown, but supposedly their presence comes with the crackle of static electricity, raising hairs on the body. Perhaps they bring the rain, or maybe they are side effects of the supposed magic that causes the storms.

Some Weathermakers (clergy of Thongfa, the deity of air) claim that Baoyan, god of fire and Thongfa's sibling, magically dried out Crackbeak to imprison Thongfa. The changing climate due to Baoyan's power altered the atmosphere, trapping dry air in a heat dome above the desert. Baoyan then used the people's prayers for rain to lure Thongfa to the region and strand her there. She offered a boon to the adventurers who freed her, and they asked for an oasis to save the people from losing their homes to the desert. Though she couldn't save Crackbeak from its fate, she created the Drowned Jungle as an alternate refuge. This religious theory is the only one that claims both the desert and jungle are magical rather than simply one or the other, and thus, it is my favorite. Acts of deities are just so much more exciting stories than "geological forces" or whatnot.

Another tale revolves around mortals attempts to defy their fate or reach out to grasp more than they deserve. Jirigati descendants whisper about a hardriggan inventor named Cholpekka (male hardriggan inventor) who sought to harness the Eld to bring rain to Crackbeak. He scoured the island for places where water elements were strong and tried to convince the eldamon there to relocate to the desert, bringing their vitality with them. The eldamon couldn't help, for they followed the changes in the world instead of directly influencing them.

After his failure with the eldamon, Cholpekka decided to embody the water element himself and directly become the change he sought. He devised a ritual to drown his mortal body and resurrect his spirit as a water eldamon. Performed in the heart of the Drowned Jungle, where the element of water was strong, the ritual transformed him but subjected him to the same restrictions, trapping him in the jungle forever.

In this telling, he remains a powerful influence on the jungle. The rain constitutes his tears, and the thunder his rage at his failure. Other variants of this story blame Cholpekka for the desert in the first place, taking the form of a lesson to be glad for what you have rather than constantly yearning for more. In these tales, Cholpekka sought the power to add even greater prosperity to the still-thriving region than before but failed and angered the water eldamon, who abandoned the region in droves, leading to the Crackbeak salt desert's formation.

People And Culture

The only respite from the jungle is Pliticori Village, a secretive galtzagorri settlement founded and governed by Peerless Ruby, a vermilion dragon (female vermilion dragon storyteller). The village is friendly to those they deem trustworthy, but the dangers of the jungle make them wary of engaging with just anyone. Only those they deem trustworthy will they approach and invite into their village. Peerless Ruby doesn't hesitate to take out perceived threats to the village, but any adventurers who enjoy good stories and have some of their own to tell, embellished or not, will find her to be a gregarious host.

Bacoo also live in the jungle, but they aren't your typical friendly mischief-makers. These antisocial rogues retreated to the jungle after becoming disenchanted with civilization, stalking the jungle, and treating all creatures mentally and physically as prey. Travelers who find themselves overpowered by these bacoo can survive by promising to bring them bigger prey for them to torment instead. Still, the reprieve lasts only as long as the travelers remain useful and can continue to deliver wicked delights to the bacoo. All the while, the bacoo harass their victims, ridiculing them and playing cruel pranks on them before finally striking them down once they tire of these antics.

Points Of Interest

Cholpekka's rumored resting place is one of the marshes collectively known as the Draught, located in the northern reaches of the Drowned Jungle. The area is a bacoo stronghold, so verifying the truth of the Jirigati myth is a dangerous endeavor. If true, however, placating Cholpekka could alter the Drowned Jungle's ecosystem and make it safer to explore.

The Draught is not the most dangerous place in the jungle, however. That designation belongs to the Petrified Palm, which lies in the shadow of Mount Rhea, north of Mount Echo. The Palm is a towering, oversized palm tree that has somehow been transformed into shale rock. Radiating necromantic energy, it corrupts nearby creatures into undead thralls crusted with shale rock. An entire ecosystem of shale fungi, birds, insects, and mammals lives on and in the tree. These shale creatures are aggressive and would likely spread their corruption if not kept in check by the magical rain, which seems to cause them harm and drives them to shelter under the Petrified Palm's leaves. Hence, the Palm's denizens are kept in check by the jungle itself, but the threat of corruption always remains.

Finally, if you push through the jungle to the coast, you might encounter the village of Molmist. While I could also write extensively about Molmist, I've been told that my descriptions of the townsfolk are too colorful. It isn't my fault people can't take criticism, but getting chased out of town for pointing out the inconsistencies in their traditions and norms isn't conducive to providing in- depth coverage of the location. Therefore, I pass you off into the capable hands of local explorers and their descriptions of the town.

Emerald Hills and Sunrise Beach.name: 翡翠丘陵与日出海滩 Emerald Hills and Sunrise Beach
Emerald Hills and Sunrise Beach.text:
“在鲜亮的色泽与晨露之间，生命在他们的翡翠面庞上闪耀。每天，塔恩·达特再次修剪群丘，抛光大地，使之映照她的恩泽。”格玛尤诗人奈伊阿娜·苏内伊如此描写翡翠丘陵，遐想其葱郁之美必定促成了它们的得名。

然而，美丽之下潜藏着危险。翡翠丘陵中唯一的文明痕迹，是愚蠢的巨龙威勒姆——失败聚居地法光的建筑师——所建的废弃渡槽，如今大半已成废墟。与溺亡丛林不同，丘陵中没有可怖的生物或令人望而生畏的环境扼杀生命。聚居地的敌人是大地本身。岩浆热点使地面不断起伏升降，让这些丘陵如同沸水之上的泡沫般短暂。丘陵不会在你眼前立刻移动，因为它们移动得很慢；但它们终究会移动，因此很少有永久结构能长久保持“永久”。

如此变动的环境与班诺格及其奇幻天性完美契合。这些兔形生物起源于梦境维度，胆小羞怯，偏爱那些能如同家乡位面的可塑梦境景观一般被它们塑形的环境。翡翠丘陵满足两项需求：既没有任何捕食者，也没有其他族裔的竞争者，同时又提供一片美丽却不断变化的景致供它们探索。挖掘地穴仍比班诺格希望的更费体力，但它们乐于规划迷宫般的地道，并利用岩浆热点加速地质变化。像威勒姆渡槽这样的永久建造尝试，往往会遭遇班诺格为驱赶建造者而制造的意外塌陷坑——如果丘陵本身尚未先行大闹一场的话。

当你继续向东北前行，翡翠丘陵便翻滚延展至日出海滩。这段海岸线确实能提供令人屏息的日出景观，但其得名的另一个灵感或许来自沙滩的条带色彩。丘陵先溶解成黑沙，越接近海洋就越泛绿，随后转为黄色，最终变成白色。这种渐变源自岛屿火山岩的侵蚀，以及被其他岛屿洋流冲刷上岸的沙粒。

侵蚀与持续的地震活动意味着海滩每天都在改变外观。每一次日出都会为海岸带来新的形状与颜色，使海滩如天空般美丽。此外，来自丘陵的多种动物与来自海的海中生物在此交汇，使其成为班诺格与乔科里都喜爱的狩猎场。

人群与文化

班诺格是翡翠丘陵中唯一值得一提的永久居民，其存在也主导着日出海滩（以及乔科里——他们喜欢海滩的景致与狩猎）。尽管害怕陌生人，当地班诺格在察觉不到威胁时也会很快放下戒心。有些班诺格甚至担任其社群的大使，接近从法光与海妖之地冒险进入丘陵的海盗与冒险者，以判断他们的意图。这些大使被称为跃梦者，因为他们通过在接近其领地之人的梦中跳跃来侦察。跃梦者起初在阴影中评估访客的潜意识，在梦境景观中嗅探麻烦。若访客通过初步测试，跃梦者便会在其目标的梦中现身，与其交谈意图。只有会面成功后，跃梦者才会在物质位面显露真身。健足尼科（无性别班诺格斥候）在数十年前为其“越界者”社群建立了这套以梦境接触新来者的流程，并且常常亲自现身欢迎访客。

在尼科与其族群最初定居翡翠丘陵后，这一初始群体多年来分裂成许多截然不同的团体。许多班诺格认为丘陵理想，便建立了新的地穴；另一些则决定与原本就存在的那支小型班诺格守望者社群一同安家。其余者继续在大地上旅行，由梦魇猎手莫甘娜（女性班诺格守望者）带领，并大约每年绕回翡翠丘陵一次。家族会在此期间举办重寻梦境之宴来庆祝重聚，丘陵各处乃至更远之地的班诺格都会前来参与。盛宴是一场为期一周的派对，其特色在于大量使用魔法来创造幻象，讲述莫甘娜旅途中的故事。本地区任何关于幽灵（特征）目击、怪异声响与骤然温度变化的报告，很可能都可归因于这场盛宴；而它每年举行的日期都不相同。

兴趣点

不断变化的地形意味着这里几乎没有永久的兴趣点。巨口便是其中之一。这个塌陷坑位于翡翠丘陵西北部靠海地段，几乎笔直向下通往大地下方深处的熔岩坑。一股微弱烟雾的永久柱状烟流自其中升起，偶尔当海水流触及其口部时会变得浓厚而洁白。当地人给它起了个绰号：鲍焱之潜。尽管名字花哨，其边缘并不稳固且相当危险，最好避开。

更靠近日出海滩、奥克山以北处是巢林。这片树林生长得足够繁密，以至于当其下方的山丘坍塌时，纠缠的树根仍将它们固定在原处。如今，树根在真正的林冠之下又形成了一层令人惊叹的穹顶。这里栖息着多种陆生与鸟类生物，以及一群名为“搜救”的巴库欧，负责照看这片林地。正如其名，他们在翡翠丘陵巡逻，寻找陷入困境的旅行者，并会迅速回应求助请求。典型行动包括救出跌入塌陷坑的徒步者，但有时当地穴意外坍塌时，其他班诺格也需要他们的帮助。不过，搜救并不排斥你可能会联想到其他巴库欧的恶作剧，他们也以假装跌落或假装把正在救的人摔下去来吓唬旁观者而闻名。

再者便是威勒姆的蠢事，也就是法光城。它坐落于金穗地最北端，那里的土地更为稳定。我曾在其街道上上演过一出“单-巨龙末世后沉浸式戏剧”，讲述一个世界里半数人口突然从存在中消失的故事。唉，当地人并不买账，且以出人意料的侵略性作出回应。我想，如果你想知道这座闹鬼且几近废弃之城的最新流言，就得去问别人了。

"With a vibrant hue and morning dew, life sparkles through their emerald faces. Each day Than Dat cuts the hills anew, polishing the earth to reflect her graces." So wrote g'mayun poet Nai'ana Sunei about the Emerald Hills, musing that their verdant beauty must have led to their name.

However, under that beauty lurks danger. The only hint of civilization in the Emerald Hills is the abandoned aqueduct of the foolish dragon Willerm—architect of the failed settlement of Farlight—which lies mainly in ruins. Unlike the Drowned Jungle, no terrifying creatures or forbidding environment throttles life in the hills. The enemy of settlements is the earth itself. Magma hotspots cause the ground to continuously rise and fall, making the hills as ephemeral as bubbles atop boiling water. The hills won't move before your eyes as they move slowly, but move they will, so few permanent structures remain permanent for long.

Such a shifting environment is a perfect fit for bannogs and their fantastical nature. Originating from the Dimension of Dreams, these rabbit-like creatures are timid and favor environments they can shape like the malleable dreamscapes of their home plane. The Emerald Hills fit both requirements, lacking any predators or rivals of other ancestries while providing a beautiful but constantly changing landscape for them to explore. Digging warrens still requires more physical exertion than bannogs would like, but they delight in planning the maze-like tunnels while using magma hotspots to accelerate geologic changes. Attempts at permanent construction, such as Willerm's aqueduct, often encounter unexpected sinkholes created by bannogs to drive them away if the hills don't wreak havoc first.

As you continue to the northeast, the Emerald Hills roll into Sunrise Beach. This shoreline does provide breathtaking views of sunrises, but another inspiration for its name may be the striated colors of its sand. The hills first dissolve into black sand, which tinges green the closer it gets to the ocean, before turning yellow and then white. This gradient results from the erosion of the island's volcanic rocks and sand that other islands' currents have washed ashore.

Erosion and continued seismic activity mean the beach changes its appearance daily. Each sunrise brings new shapes and colors to the coast, making the beach as beautiful as the sky. Plus, a wide variety of animals from the hills mix with sea life from the sea, making it a popular hunting ground for bannogs as well as chochori.

People And Culture

Bannogs are the only permanent population of note in the Emerald Hills, and their presence also dominates Sunrise Beach (along with chochori, who enjoy the sights and hunting along the beach). Though fearful of strangers, the local bannogs warm up quickly when they perceive no threat. Some bannogs even act as ambassadors for their communities, approaching the pirates and adventurers who venture into the hills from Farlight and Kraken to determine their intentions. These ambassadors are called hoppers because they scout by hopping through the dreams of people who approach their lands. Hoppers initially evaluate a visitor's subconscious from the shadows, sniffing the dreamscape for trouble. If they pass their initial tests, hoppers appear in their targets' dreams and chat with them about their intentions. Only after a successful meeting do hoppers reveal themselves on the Material Plane. Sure-Footed Nikko (nonbinary bannog scout) established this dreaming contact process for newcomers for his community of bounders several decades ago and often welcomes visitors in the flesh.

After Nikko and his colony initially settled in the Emerald Hills, this initial group split into many distinct groups over the years. Many found the Hills ideal and set up new burrows, while others decided to settle down with the small bannog warden community already present. The rest continue to travel the land, led by Nightmare Slayer Morgana (female bannog warden), and circle back to the Emerald Hills about once a year. The family holds the Feast of Rediscovered Dreams during this time to celebrate their reunion, with bannog from around the Hills and beyond arriving for the celebration. The Feast is a week-long party defined by the extensive use of magic to create illusions that tell the stories of Morgana's travels. Any reports of ghost sightings, strange sounds, and sudden temperature changes in this region can likely be attributed to The Feast, which occurs on different dates each year.

Points Of Interest

The ever-changing terrain means there are few permanent points of interest in this area. The Maw is one such location. This sinkhole is in the northwest section of the Emerald Hills near the coast and leads almost straight down to a pit of lava deep below the earth. A perpetual column of faint smoke rises out of it, occasionally turning thick and white when a flow of seawater reaches its mouth. Locals nickname it Baoyan's Dive. Despite the fanciful name, the rim is unstable and quite dangerous, so it is best avoided.

Closer to Sunrise Beach and north of Mount Auk is the Nest. This grove of trees grew thick enough that their entangled roots held them in place when the hill collapsed beneath them. The roots now form an impressive canopy below the actual forest canopy. It's home to a variety of terrestrial and avian creatures, as well as a group of bacoo called Search and Rescue that tend to the grove. True to their name, they patrol the Emerald Hills for travelers in distress and are quick to respond to requests for help. Typical operations involve rescuing hikers who fall into sinkholes, but sometimes other bannogs need their help when a warren collapses unexpectedly. Search and Rescue are not above the mischievous antics you might associate with other bacoos, though, and they have a reputation for terrifying onlookers by faking a fall or pretending to drop someone they're saving.

Then there's Willerm's Folly, also known as the city of Farlight. It sits on the northernmost tip of Goldcrop, where the land is more stable. I once staged a one- dragon post-apocalyptic immersive theater production in its streets, telling a tale of a world where half the population suddenly vanished from existence. The locals were not impressed and responded with surprising aggression, alas. I suppose you'll have to ask someone else if you want the latest gossip from the haunted, nearly abandoned city.

The Golden Mountains.name: 黄金群山 The Golden Mountains
The Golden Mountains.text:
在任何一个城镇里买冬装之前先停手，免得你浪费哪怕一枚铜币！那些热带天堂的居民根本不懂真正的寒冷，所以不管裁缝的技艺多么高超，你都不可能在那种地方找到最好的寒冷天气装备。去任意一座山的山脚，你至少能找到一个迷人的矿业前哨站，那里有一位很不错的硬里根裁缝。他们以为那些不习惯寒冷的族裔提供装备为荣。灵巧的埃库卡特拉（男性硬里根裁缝），“剪羊毛吧，抖起来”的老板，店就在战栗群山脚下，他甚至还为我的尾巴织了一件定制毛衣！我绝对推荐任何能来这一趟的人去找他们。

总体而言，黄金群山由十一座山峰组成。其中最高的是令人不安的战栗山。从那里顺时针走，你会看到一对山峰名为回声山，因为你可以在其中一座上大喊，然后从另一座听到回声。接着是一组三座山，分别叫瑞亚山、金黄鹂山与唐纳雀山，合称为歌唱群山。这三座以北是俯瞰翡翠丘陵的翡翠山，之后又是一组三座合称为舞蹈群山的山：迪涅玛山、阿德莉娅山与卡萨希娅山。最后，奥克山独自矗立在东方。

黄金群山的所有山峰都是火山，但其中一些的活动明显比其他更频繁。翡翠山很久没有喷发了，但有许多温泉。其内部含有的高热量让树木能一直生长到山顶，使它在被积雪覆盖的兄弟山峰之间显得格外葱绿。舞蹈群山是最活跃的，每隔几年就会喷发一次，因此得名。其山坡已成为候鸟的栖息地，并孕育了种类繁多、能在这种独特土壤中茁壮生长的开花植物。

与此同时，歌唱群山已经很久没有任何火山活动了，因此小妖精（特征）与巴库在此定居。这些山的名字源于山坡上独特的岩石：敲击时会像铃铛一样鸣响。山坡村落把这些岩石当作乐器，既用于典礼，也用于创造真正独特的音乐声响。

战栗山是最大的一座山，却惊人地几乎没有生命。它高山森林的部分区域寂静无声，只有风吹动树木沙沙作响。关于阴影中的怪物与失踪旅人的故事更添阴郁，使人们相信它被诅咒了。数次失败的采矿远征只会让它的恶名更盛，而且不只是那些可笑的淘金者。煤与铁矿通常能在黄金群山的山脚找到——至少在当地克拉格拉克允许的地方——但若你试着在战栗山采矿，通常只会以悲剧收场。关于这座山如何得名的传奇各不相同，其中包括有人相信是波比·冯·藤壶亲自为其命名。究竟是因为其构造活动，还是因为那令人胆寒的氛围，无人能说得准。

岛上两条主要河流——触手河与迷雾河——都发源于战栗山顶的融雪。迷雾河在两条河里更小，较浅且平缓。这是一片宁静的流域，住着渔夫与农夫，支撑着拉姆普兰克贪得无厌的胃口。与此同时，触手河则更像一片未驯的荒野，因此我会在它自己的章节里更详细地介绍。

在金穗的历史上，久远之前的一次大喷发还创造了嘶鸣裂隙。这条漫长的裂谷构成了迷雾丛林与战栗丛林之间的边界。它通常是一处平静的山谷，几乎不费力就能穿越，但丛林螳螂每年会有几个月把这里当作繁殖地。虫群相当危险，出现时会让裂隙无法通行，而它们聚集时发出的愤怒噪声也让这片区域因此得名。

人民与文化

并不令人意外，克拉格拉克是居住在黄金山脉各个山峰之间数量最为庞大的族群。大大小小的克拉格拉克聚居地遍布山坡，但这些聚居地整体上松散地分为三种截然不同的文化认同。

库洛武特克拉格拉克是三者中人数最多的一支。他们顽固而骄傲，是传统主义者，崇拜埃尔德，并与多种古老的德鲁伊仪式与习俗保持紧密联系。他们运营着蜿蜒深入火山的隧道，以引导其中强大而动态的元素之力，并监管深入这些火山隧道的成年仪式，以确保万无一失。舞蹈群山是他们的据点。

图科图尔克拉格拉克主要是建造者与发明家，大多居住在回声山与奥克山。高耸的石质建筑、并且仍与自然保持和谐，是他们创新设计的标志。图科图尔不断努力去设计可持续的方案，以解决建筑、经济与社会方面的难题，并将劳动成果分享给外界，使其他文化不至于被诱导去选择更具环境破坏性的道路。

克拉格拉克中最小的一支——差距相当明显——是佩格普瑞尔，其成员追随三位时代姐妹，要么分别信奉，要么把她们作为“时代同胞”万神殿整体来崇拜。许多其他克拉格拉克认为佩格普瑞尔不过是个邪教，当然，他们绝不会当着佩格普瑞尔邻居的面这么说。据说三位时间神祇曾对现代佩格普瑞尔的祖先显灵，揭示了金穗注定要在整个阿拉卡尔世界的历史中扮演某种所谓“关键”的角色。因此，借由与她们女神的盟约，这支克拉格拉克群体持续守夜着附近的土地。他们谨慎观察，等待那一日到来，并希望能参与其中，协助金穗及其居民挺身面对这一命定之日的挑战，无论那一天会在何时到来。自然，我们其他人都知道这则佩格普瑞尔童话大概毫无可能为真。因此，用一些善意的玩笑与揶揄去戳戳他们的自命不凡，也不失为一种乐趣！佩格普瑞尔把大部分时间用于深度冥想，并声称他们由此获得的洞见能帮助他们引导他人走向各自的命运。尽管佩格普瑞尔的行动有时难以捉摸，但无视他们的宣告或许并不明智——山中确实存在几次可信的目击，声称见到了德瑞米拉、阿万娜与赫斯特，因此这些神祇也许确实对这座岛有些兴趣，只不过未必像佩格普瑞尔宣称的那般夸大。

在黄金群山中，硬里根的人口数量仅次于克拉格拉克。然而，该地区的硬里根文化几乎与山地聚居地中以社群为导向的克拉格拉克群体截然相反。这里的黄金山硬里根高度个人主义，并为各自的个人成就感到骄傲；他们在此地没有永久聚居地，但会以狩猎团的友谊群体形式聚集，协力追踪并猎杀大型而危险的猎物，同时也会组建典型的硬里根支柱，由其中最强、最凶猛、最有野心者领导。尽管该地区也有一些硬里根安定下来，建起自己的小屋或家园，但更多人会随心所欲地迁徙往来，以适应他们的兴致、当下需求以及季节更迭。

由于他们游牧且个人主义的天性，若非每年一次的“狂奔”——他们最珍视的根源——想要哪怕粗略估算山中有多少硬里根都几乎不可能。这场穿越群山的竞速在隆冬之巅举行，既像节庆、也像聚会，但主要是一场盛大的竞赛。鉴于几乎没有硬里根能拒绝挑战，几乎整个山地区域的每一位硬里根都会现身狂奔，要么参赛，要么作为观众在一旁观看。狂奔的参赛者必须沿着一条特定路线前进；路线每年都会变化，蜿蜒穿过黄金群山的全部十一座山峰，以争取夺冠后伴随一生的吹嘘资本。任何人都能参加狂奔，无论是硬里根还是其他任何族裔，但根源鼓励年轻硬里根先花上几年当观众，而年长硬里根则沿线站位，为参赛者提供支援，并为所有人执行竞赛规则。曾经的冠军被严禁再次参赛。取而代之的是，所有前冠军每年会共同设计新的路线。当然，为了测试赛道的可行性与难度，这些挑战建筑师必须在对外开放之前先自行跑完全程。

山顶终年严寒，使冬之神卡萨的崇拜以一种在夏意盎然的靛蓝群岛其他地方不太常见的方式渗透进文化之中。大多数人并不同意卡萨更严苛的观点，但他们把他“做好准备”的典范铭记于心。许多文化都有一种根源：人们自愿让自己承受困苦，以测试自身的韧性，希望自己的行为能安抚卡萨，并说服他不要降下属于他自己的试炼。村落甚至可能宣传此类活动，因为据说这会吸引来自山谷的索特尔索特尔，他们寻求这种严酷体验以提升自己的生存（技能）。

兴趣点

黄金群山中最密集的人口中心并不位于群山之上，而是如人所料，坐落于群山之间的山谷中。当然，这些金色山谷的海拔仍高于岛屿其他大部分地区，并且它们的气候会在一年之中、乃至日与日之间显著波动，因为来自山上的冷空气会滞留其中。这里坐落着山中最接近“硬里根城镇”的地方：凯尔戈尔训练场。这是一处强度极高的障碍路线，常住着一小群法师，他们会改变地形并创造陷阱来挑战访客。该地区也被视为中立之地，供山里的硬里根通过非致命战斗或体能竞赛来解决争端。其他族裔也欢迎前来，但别指望挑战会为他们而降低难度。

想要更放松的人应当去打滚之水——位于翡翠山上的一处温泉浴场，由名为安纳乌与贝勒芭的一对奥波克夫妻经营。它拥有拉姆普兰克最顶级度假村的一切奢华，却被美丽的荒野所环绕，而不是被醉醺醺的城市人包围。拒绝进行任何休整模式的冒险者也仍应考虑前往一趟，以调查该地区周期性弥漫的诡异浓雾。这一现象伴随着非自然的寒冷，甚至让流入温泉的水都变得冰凉。本次事件的元凶似乎具有秘法性质，不过也有人说其中也有神圣魔法的迹象。

如果你信奉任何一位时间女神，你一定应该考虑造访时代隐修院——它被严密守卫，坐落在唐纳雀山的山巅。佩格普瑞尔克拉格拉克只允许三位时间同胞的牧师进入，但据传其中藏有一件魔法奇物，能显现过往事件与可能未来的异象。许多历史学家曾尝试进入隐修院以揭开靛蓝群岛笼罩的过往，但都以失败告终。

若这些选择都不足以满足你对热量的偏好，不妨加入一支库洛武特火焰旅，探索深藏于群山之下的火山隧道。火山内部的炽热会让物质界与火位面之间的边界变得稀薄。许多生物生活在岩浆中，从熔融九头蛇到火焰潜行者，并会游荡到山坡上搜寻猎物。火焰旅守护群山安全，避免不得不塌陷隧道来把威胁困在其中——那会扰乱克拉格拉克的根源。旅队掌握着特殊技巧，其中许多会利用埃尔德来驾驭大地之力，以安全而人道的方式让这些生物保持在可控范围内。

Stop before you waste a single copper buying winter clothing in one of the towns! Denizens of those tropical paradises don't know real cold, so no matter how skilled the tailor, you'll never find the best cold-weather gear if that's where you're looking for it. Go to the base of any of the mountains, and you'll find at least one charming mining outpost with a good hardriggan tailor. They take great pride in outfitting ancestries less accustomed to the cold. Ekukkatra the Dextrous (male hardriggan tailor), owner of Shiver Me Shearers at the foot of the Shivering Mountains, even knitted me a custom sweater for my tail! I definitely recommend them to anyone who can make the trip.

Eleven peaks make up the Golden Mountains as a whole. The tallest of them all is the foreboding Shivering Mountain. Going clockwise from there, you'll find a pair of peaks called Mount Echo because you can yell from one and hear an echo back from the other. Then there's a trio of mountains named Rhea, Oriole, and Tanager, collectively known as the Singing Mountains. North of those three is the Emerald Mountain overlooking the Emerald Hills, followed by another trio called the Dancing Mountains: Dinema, Adelia, and Casasia. Finally, Mount Auk stands on its own to the east.

All the peaks in the Golden Mountains are volcanoes, but some of them see significantly more activity than others. Emerald Mountain hasn't erupted in a long time but has many hot springs. Its high heat content allows trees to grow all the way to its summit, causing it to stand out as green among its snow-covered siblings. The Dancing Mountains are the most active and erupt every few years, giving them their name. While the slopes have become home to migratory birds and host a wide variety of flowering plants that thrive in the unique soil.

Meanwhile, the Singing Mountains haven't seen any volcanic activity in quite some time, leading to settlement by sprites and bacoo. These mountains gained their name from unique rocks on their slopes that ring like bells when struck. The villages on the mountains' slopes use these rocks as instruments both for ceremonies and to create truly unique musical sounds.

Shivering Mountain is the largest mountain and is surprisingly devoid of life. Parts of its alpine forests are quiet, except for the wind rustling the trees. Tales of shadowy monsters and disappearing travelers add to the gloom, leading to the belief that it's cursed. Several failed mining expeditions only add to its reputation, and not just the silly ones searching for gold. Coal and iron ore mines are commonly found at the Golden Mountains' bases—at least where permitted by local kragraks—but try your luck mining the Shivering Mountain, which usually tends to end in tragedy. Legends vary as to how the mountain got its name, including those who believe that Poppy von Barnacle herself named it. Whether for its tectonic activity or terrifying ambiance, no one can say for sure.

The two major rivers on the island, the Tentacle River and the Misty River, both originate from snowmelt atop Shivering Mountain. The Misty River is the smaller of the two rivers, relatively shallow and calm. It's a peaceful watershed populated by fishers and farmers who support Rumplank's voracious appetite. Meanwhile, the Tentacle River is much more of an untamed wilderness, so I'll cover it in more detail in its own section.

A major eruption long ago in Goldcrop's history also created Hissing Chasm. This long rift forms the border between the Misty Jungle and the Shivering Jungle. It's usually a calm valley that requires little effort to cross, but jungle mantises use it as a breeding ground for a few months every year. The swarms are quite dangerous, making the chasm impassable when present, and the angry noises they make when they gather give the area its name.

People And Culture

Unsurprisingly, perhaps, kragraks are by far the most populous peoples to be found living among the various peaks of the Golden Mountain range. Kragrak settlements large and small dot the mountainsides, but all these settlements are loosely organized into three distinct cultural identities.

Kulovut kragraks are the most populous of the three groups. Stubborn and proud traditionalists, they worship the Eld and maintain a strong connection to a variety of ancient druidic rites and customs. They operate tunnels that wind deep into the volcanoes in order to channel the force of the powerful dynamic elements within and oversee coming-of-age rituals delving deep within those volcanic tunnels to ensure that nothing goes wrong. The Dancing Mountains are their stronghold.

Turkotur kragraks are predominantly builders and inventors who primarily populate Mount Echo and Mount Auk. Soaring stone architecture that remains in harmony with nature is a hallmark of their innovative designs. Turkotur constantly strives to engineer sustainable solutions to architectural, economic, and societal conundrums and then to share the fruits of their labor with the outside world so that other cultures aren't tempted to choose more environmentally destructive paths.

The smallest group of kragrak, by a significant margin, is Pergprel, whose members follow the Three Sisters of Time, either individually or as the Siblings of Time pantheon as a whole. Many other kragraks see the Pergprel group as little more than a cult, though of course they would never say so directly to the faces of their Pergprel neighbors. The three temporal deities supposedly spoke to the ancestors of modern- day Pergprel and revealed some supposed "pivotal" role that Goldcrop is meant to play in the history of the entire world of Alacar. Thus, via a covenant with their goddesses, this kragrak group continuously watches over the land nearby. They observe carefully, waiting for this day to come and hoping to participate and help Goldcrop and its inhabitants rise to face the challenge of this destined day, no matter when that day might be. The rest of us know that there's roughly no chance that this Pergprel fairytale is true, though, naturally. And so, of course, it can be amusing to poke fun at their self-importance with some good-natured jokes and jibes! Pergprels spend most of their time in deep meditation and claim that the insights they gain while doing so help them guide others to their own destinies. While the actions of Pergprels are inscrutable at times, ignoring their proclamations might not be wise—a few credible sightings exist of Dremira, Awanna, and Herst in the mountains, so they may indeed have some interest in this island, even if not as overblown as Pergprel claims make it out to be.

Hardriggans make up the second-largest population in the Golden Mountains after kragraks. However, hardriggan culture in the region is nearly the opposite of that of the community-oriented kragrak groups found among their mountain settlements. Highly individualistic and proud of each of their personal achievements, these Golden Mountain hardriggans have no permanent settlements in the area, but they do gather together in hunting band friendship groups that work together to stalk and take down large and dangerous prey, in addition to forming typical hardriggan Pillars, led by the strongest, fiercest, and most ambitious among them. While some hardriggans in the region settle down and build a cabin or homestead of their own, many move from place to place to suit their whims, their current needs, and the changing of the seasons.

Because of their itinerant and individualistic nature, knowing even roughly how many hardriggans live in the mountains would be nearly impossible if not for the annual Stampede, their most cherished tradition. Part festival, part meetup, but mostly a grand competition, this race through the mountains occurs at the height of winter. Given that few hardriggans can possibly pass up a challenge, almost every hardriggan in the entire mountain region shows up to the Stampede, either to partake in the race or to watch from the sidelines as a spectator. Participants of the Stampede must navigate a specific route, which changes from year to year, winding through all eleven peaks of the Golden Mountains in order to claim the lifetime bragging rights that come with finishing in first place. Anyone can enter the Stampede, be they hardriggan or of any other ancestry, but tradition encourages young hardriggans to spend a few years spectating while older hardriggans line the route to support racers and enforce the rules of the competition for all. Former winners are strictly forbidden from competing a second time. Instead, all the former winners collaborate to design the new route each year. Of course, to test the course's viability and difficulty, the challenge architects must complete the course themselves before opening it up to challengers.

The perpetual cold atop the mountains means worship of Kasa, the god of winter, permeates the culture in a way that isn't seen much elsewhere in the summery Indigo Isles. Most don't agree with Kasa's harsher perspectives, but they take to heart his edict to be prepared. Many cultures have traditions where people voluntarily bring hardship upon themselves to test their resilience, hoping that their actions placate Kasa and convince him not to send his own trials. Villages might even advertise such events, as they are known to draw xotlxotl from the valleys who seek out such harsh experiences to improve their survival skills.

Points Of Interest

The densest population centers in the Golden Mountains aren't found astride the mountains themselves but instead, predictably, lie in the valleys between the mountains. Of course, these golden valleys are still at a higher elevation than much of the rest of the island, and their climate fluctuates significantly across the year and day to day as cold air from the mountains can settle in them. Here stands the closest thing to a hardriggan town in the mountains: the Kelgorr Training Grounds. It's an intense obstacle course with a small permanent population of mages who alter the terrain and create traps to challenge visitors. The area is also considered a neutral place for hardriggans from the mountains to settle arguments through nonlethal combat or physical competitions. Other ancestries are welcome, but they shouldn't expect challenges to be toned down for them.

Those looking for a more relaxing time should visit Wallowing Waters, a spa on Emerald Mountain owned by an orpok couple named Annawu and Belleba. It has all the luxuries of the best resorts in Rumplank while being surrounded by beautiful wilderness instead of drunk cityfolk. Adventurers who refuse to take any downtime should still consider a visit to investigate the strange fog that periodically settles in the area. Unnatural cold accompanies the phenomenon, making even the waters running into the hot springs turn chilly. The culprit in this instance seems to be occult in nature, though some say there are hints of divine magic at play as well.

If you're a worshiper of any of the goddesses of time, you should definitely consider visiting the heavily guarded Cloister of Time atop the peak of Mount Tanager. Pergprel kragraks only allow clerics of the Three Siblings to enter, but it's rumored to contain a magical artifact that shows visions of past events and possible futures. Many historians have tried and failed to access the cloister to shed light on the Indigo Isles' shrouded past.

If none of these options provide enough heat to satisfy your predilections, consider joining a Kulovot flame brigade to explore the volcanic tunnels deep under the mountains. The intense heat inside volcanoes thins the boundaries between the Material Plane and the Plane of Fire. Many creatures live in the magma, from molten hydras to fire prowlers, and wander onto the slopes searching for prey. Flame brigades keep the mountains safe and prevent the need to collapse tunnels to trap threats inside, which would disrupt kragrak traditions. The brigades have special techniques at their disposal, many of which utilize the Eld to harness the powers of the earth to safely and humanly keep these creatures at bay.

The Shivering Jungle and the Hook.name: 颤抖的丛林与钩 The Shivering Jungle and the Hook
The Shivering Jungle and the Hook.text:
在“颤栗丛林”里发抖可不是什么好兆头。这片低洼沼泽地很少变冷，也几乎不经历地震活动。所以如果你开始发抖？多半是疾病所致。这里蚊子虫群充斥空气，传播疟疾与其他热病，而沼泽水有时还会携带霍乱。带上药物，或——更妙的是——用油或法术把这些嗡嗡作响的威胁彻底驱离。毫无准备的访客若闹出足够大的动静，可能会引来当地巴库（bacoo）的注意。与颤栗丛林的巴库交易换取疗方，总比白拿一个要好——他们在出售货物时往往很讲荣誉，但在施舍时却爱恶作剧，所以那份免费的驱蚊剂确实会生效，但可能会让你的皮肤颜色变一天，或让你长出短暂的角。浓密的红树林沿海岸延伸，提供抵御风暴与侵蚀的保护，而海生物在其根系与附近礁石间繁衍兴盛。这里是朗普兰克（Rumplank）和克拉肯（Kraken）居民喜爱的垂钓地——只是别要求和当地人同行，他们会嫉妒地守着自己的秘密钓点不放。尽管如此，没有什么比在阳光下躺在鱼线旁、看海牛与海豚在近处嬉戏更能让人感到天堂般惬意了。寻找盘旋的海鸥来判断鱼群聚集之处，或聆听鲸歌来定位它们的觅食水域。

咸水确实会侵入该区域，因此水体在向内陆延伸数英里内都呈半咸水。这种独特的环境孕育了多种别处找不到的水栖动物（特征），以及能同时在淡水与咸水环境中生存的动物，如鳗鱼、螃蟹与凯门鳄。这里也栖息着泥鳄（mudgator）——两栖短吻鳄，能像在水中游动一样轻易地在泥里掘进。它们会从看似坚实的地面跃出伏击猎物，极其危险。探索此地时务必带上你的十英尺长杆！

然而，颤栗丛林里含盐的地带反而是沼泽中更适宜生存的部分。在淡水占优势的地方，任何带着过滤工具包与狩猎技能的人都能无限期地活下去。这也意味着内陆地区成了逃亡者与其他不法之徒的热门去处。其中也有友善的隐士，但以他们的社交偏好而言，你遇到的任何人都不太可能是那种。

臭名昭著的海盗血喙（女性 g'mayun 海盗）在十年前行刑前夜逃出牢房后，便消失在颤栗丛林中。她残余的船员与同伙也在同一夜失踪，据说是追随她进入丛林。传言她仍在那片丛林里，正在招募船员并组装一艘船，准备再次出海。金穗地（Goldcrop）各处常有乐于助人的陌生人，但在丛林里要对他们保持警惕——你可能会被抓壮丁塞进海盗船员里，或更糟。

在南方颤栗丛林与嘶嘶裂隙（Hissing Chasm）交汇之处，传闻栖息着一种名为高跷行者（Stiltwalker）的神秘生物。据说它形似麋鹿，却有十英尺长的腿，头颅是鸟的骷髅，且其上长出蠕动的鹿角；不过按理性推断，这更可能是某位居民用来阻止人们频繁接近该区域的伎俩。也有人从颤栗丛林的支流处被水冲入触手河（Tentacle River）的尸体，其身上的穿刺孔与任何已知生物都不相符，连嗜血蚊（bloodseeker）也不是。这些孔洞能贯穿骨骼，因此人们将凶手称为吸髓者（Marrowsucker）。

钩（The Hook）是一处位于颤栗丛林以南的半岛。尽管在地理形态上与周围沼泽地并无明显区别，但它因造成船难而得名。该区域洋流强劲，低洼地形会让缺乏经验的船长误以为能抄近路穿过看似开阔的水面，从而搁浅。随着沉船不断累积，这个问题还在恶化——残骸把原本安全的航线变成遍布木料的雷区，吸引更多船只前来，想从沉船中打捞其宝藏。

人物与文化

金穗地的大多数 xotlxotl 人口居住在颤栗丛林中。正是与其危险共存的挑战，让这里有了家的感觉。各村落都有一项成年仪式：要求年轻人独自朝圣前往海岸，并带回一件来自足够危险的掠食者的战利品。多数人选择鲨鱼牙，村落间的小径两旁因此遍布鲨牙。

著名的 xotlxotl 贤者，外来者称其为预言者（Predictor，非二元 xotlxotl 贤者），居住在颤栗山（Shivering Mountain）东南方、一座从沼泽中隆起的岛屿上。他们是“无知之学”的学生，运用对人、炼金术与星象的知识来预测将会发生的事件，以及其祈求者应如何应对。人们从靛蓝群岛（Indigo Isles）以外的地方前来，向其咨询从个人疾病到国家间外交等同样重大的议题。预言者不要求任何报酬来交换其服务，只因有机会磨炼技艺而感到满足——不过多数人仍会慷慨捐赠给他们的村落。然而，受其自身族裔对“严厉之爱”的观点影响，预言者不会直接给其他 xotlxotl 答案，而是以尖锐问题挑战他们，直到他们找出自己的道路。

他们在各方面追求完美的专注，使 xotlxotl 通过反复试错发展出独特做法。其中最有趣的是沼泽医术。其理论认为：皮肤是生物体内最大的器官，因此是健康的核心。敷用正确的膏药、油剂或活体生物，既能治疗疾病，也能促进身心强健。证据显示这些疗法确实有效，但病人往往在意识到究竟要被涂抹上什么之后便望而却步。这些混合物恶臭难闻，滴着沼泽泥浆，充满蠕动的昆虫；有时像冰块般冰冷滑腻，有时又像腐肉般温热潮湿。

颤栗丛林的巴库往往不与 xotlxotl 混居，认为他们的社交习俗既可笑又令人不适。巴库是颤栗丛林中第二大的群体，但主要集中在沿海地带。关于他们的名声存在一种奇怪的二分：他们既被视为害虫，也被视为理想盟友。多数会与既有人口中心打交道的巴库往往属于前者——在不合时宜时耍把戏，或纠缠人们直到得到他们想要的食物与饮品，通常是牛奶、朗姆酒或香蕉。更庞大的群体则与世无争，或只在感召驱使下才与其他族裔合作。那类互动往往无私且高效，塑造了“英雄巴库”的神秘形象：为他们认为值得的人挺身而出、力挽狂澜。现实是，巴库与任何其他族裔一样拥有多样的性格与观点，但多数巴库似乎乐见这些关于他们的叙事持续流传。

兴趣点

触手河最长的支流之一注入半咸水的奥卡卡乌欧欧湖（Lake Ookakauoo），这是颤栗丛林中最受欢迎的目的地。它拥有独特的生态系统，也是别处找不到的美味篮蛤的栖息地。来自其他岛屿的贸易船只最近意外将礁爪（reefclaw）的卵带入此地，这些入侵生物正在造成严重破坏。它们不仅会积极狩猎本地物种，还会破坏能阻止更多咸水进一步侵入湖中的植物。不同派系间的冲突，以及“狩猎一种具备一定智慧的物种”所引发的道德困境，阻碍了任何人有效推进问题解决；但若有冒险者解决此事，将能从所有相关方获得奖励。

想雇佣巴库从事佣兵工作的，应前往沿海三处采集点之一。这些地点看起来不像村落，因为尽管巴库永远懒散地待在树枝上，却没有任何他们在此居住的迹象。疾走礁石（Scuttle Reef）靠近从钩延伸出去的其中一座岛屿。顾名思义，疾走巴库（scuttle bacoo）在此最为常见。他们会为想从沉船中回收物体的人担任向导或拾荒者。从疾走礁石沿海岸向克拉肯方向走到半途，是“红树林之母”——一棵巨大的红树，高耸于其他红树之上，也是多种巴库的居所。这里的巴库往往是熟练水手，始终被海盗船队高度需求。船只会前来竞争，完成那些他们想招募的对象提出的任务，以证明自己最配得上这些熟练巴库的服务。最后，香蕉林（Banana Grove）位于克拉肯以南约半天路程，深受寻求医疗援助或希望研究冷门学识的人欢迎。

最后，“珍娜之爱”号的残骸（wreck of Jenna's Love）必定会让任何冒险者心跳加速。你可能会惊讶地发现：该区域最著名的沉船并不在钩的海岸外。不是的——它在内陆数英里处！尽管海盗常开玩笑说颤栗丛林沼泽多到能把船直接开过去，但那是夸张。没人知道这艘残骸如何来到如今的位置——不仅搁浅，还被一棵巨大的枯柏树贯穿钉死。它紧紧守着自己的秘密，因为船上闹鬼：一支凶残的 g'mayun 船员亡灵会在见到入侵者时立刻发动攻击。附近的 xotlxotl 会把这里当作试炼场，测试自己能深入废墟到何种程度，才会被迫撤退。传说这艘船曾运送一件强大到不可思议的魔法遗物，其守卫甚至在死亡后仍决意守护其所押运之物。迄今为止，尚无人真正抵达货舱；不过据说许多尝试失败的人，已加入那支不死船员，一同守护其秘密。

A shiver is a bad sign in the Shivering Jungle. These low- lying marshes are rarely cold, nor do they experience much seismic activity. So if you begin to shiver? Likely a disease. Mosquito swarms fill the air here, spreading malaria and other fevers, while the swamp waters sometimes carry cholera. Bring medicine, or—better yet—use oils or spells to ward off the buzzing menaces altogether. Unprepared visitors who make a loud enough fuss might attract the attention of a local bacoo. Always trade with Shivering Jungle bacoo for a remedy rather than accept one for free—they tend to be honorable when selling goods but mischievous when providing charity, so that free mosquito repellent will work but might change your skin color for a day or give you temporary horns. Thick mangroves line the coast, providing protection from storms and erosion, while sea life thrives among their roots and nearby reefs. It's a popular fishing destination for residents of Rumplank and Kraken; just don't ask to tag along with the locals as they jealously guard their secret fishing holes. Still, nothing quite says paradise like lying in the sun beside some lines while manatees and dolphins play nearby. Search for circling gulls to find where fish have gathered, or listen for whale songs to locate their feeding grounds.

Salt water does intrude into the region, so the water is brackish for miles inland. This unique environment hosts a variety of aquatic animals not found elsewhere, in addition to animals that can survive both freshwater and saltwater environments, such as eels, crabs, and caimans. It is also home to mudgators, amphibious alligators that burrow through the mud as easily as they swim through water. They leap out of what looks like solid ground to ambush prey and are extremely dangerous. Make sure to bring your ten-foot pole with you when exploring the land!

Yet the briny parts of the Shivering Jungle are the more hospitable parts of the swamp. Where freshwater dominates, anyone with a filtration kit and hunting skills can survive indefinitely. That means the interior region is a popular destination for fugitives and other ne'er-do-wells. There are friendly hermits among the bunch, but the nature of their social preferences means anyone you meet is unlikely to be one.

When the infamous pirate Bloodbeak (female g'mayun pirate) escaped her cell the night before her execution ten years ago, she disappeared into the Shivering Jungle. Her remaining crew and accomplices also disappeared that same night, supposedly joining her in the jungle. Rumor is that she's still out there, gathering a crew and assembling a ship to take to the seas once again. Helpful strangers abound across Goldcrop, but be wary of them in the jungle, as you might get press-ganged into a pirate crew, or worse.

Where the Shivering Jungle and the Hissing Chasm meet in the south, it is rumored to be home to a cryptid called Stiltwalker. It's said to resemble a moose with ten- foot-long legs and a bird's skull for a head with wriggling antlers growing out of it, though logic suggests it is a ploy by a resident to discourage people from frequenting the area. Bodies have also washed into the Tentacle River from tributaries in the Shivering Jungle, which show puncture marks that don't match any known creature, not even bloodseekers. These holes penetrate bone, causing people to label the perpetrator Marrowsucker.

The Hook is a peninsula south of the Shivering Jungle. While not geographically distinct from the surrounding swampland, it earns its name for being the cause of shipwrecks. The currents are strong in the area, and the low-lying land causes inexperienced captains to run aground, thinking they can take a shortcut through what looks like open water. The problem continues to worsen as wrecks accumulate, turning formerly safe routes into wood-strewn minefields, which attract more ships hoping to scavenge sunken ones for their treasures.

People And Culture

Most of Goldcrop's xotlxotl population lives in the Shivering Jungle. The challenge of surviving its dangers is what makes the place feel like home. The villages have a coming-of-age ritual that requires youngsters to make a pilgrimage to the coast by themselves and bring back a trophy from a suitably dangerous predator. Most choose shark teeth, which line the paths leading to each village.

The renowned xotlxotl sage, known to outsiders as Predictor (nonbinary xotlxotl sage), lives southeast of Shivering Mountain on an island rising out of the swamp. They are a Student of Ignorance and use their knowledge of people, alchemy, and the stars to predict events that will come to pass and how their supplicants should respond to them. People travel from beyond the Indigo Isles to consult them on matters as personal as illnesses and as consequential as diplomacy between nations. Predictor demands no compensation for their services, happy for opportunities to hone their craft—though most people donate generously to their village nonetheless. Due to views of tough love among their own ancestry, however, Predictor doesn't provide answers to other xotlxotl, instead challenging them with pointed questions until they discover their own path.

Their focus on perfection in all ways has led xotlxotl to develop unique practices through trial and error. Swamp medicine is the most interesting of them. The theory goes that the skin is the largest organ on a creature and, therefore, central to health. Applying the right poultices, oils, or organisms treats illnesses while promoting a strong body and mind. The evidence indicates that their treatments work, but patients tend to balk once they realize what exactly is going to be put on them. The concoctions are foul-smelling substances, dripping with swamp mud and filled with wriggling insects, at times cold and slippery as ice cubes, other times warm and moist like rotting flesh.

Bacoo in the Shivering Jungle tend not to mix with xotlxotl, finding their social practices ridiculous and unpleasant. They are the second-largest population in the Shivering Jungle, but they're concentrated along the coast. A strange dichotomy exists regarding their reputation, as they are seen both as pests and desirable allies. Most bacoo who engage with established population centers tend to be the former, playing tricks at inconvenient times or harassing people until given their desired food and drink, typically milk, rum, or bananas. The larger population keeps to themselves or works with other ancestries only when they feel a calling to do so. Those interactions tend to be altruistic and effective, creating the mystique of the "heroic bacoo" who saves the day for those they deem worthy. The reality is that bacoo have the same diversity of personalities and perspectives as any other ancestry, but most seem satisfied with the persistence of these narratives about them.

Points Of Interest

One of the longest tributaries of the Tentacle River feeds the brackish Lake Ookakauoo, which is the most popular destination in the Shivering Jungle. It has a unique ecosystem and is home to delicious basket clams not found anywhere else. Trading ships from other islands accidentally introduced reefclaw eggs into the area recently, and the invasive creatures are wreaking havoc. They both aggressively hunt local species and damage plants that prevent additional saltwater intrusion into the lake. Conflicts between different factions, as well as the moral quandary around hunting a somewhat intelligent species, have prevented anyone from making headway on the problem, but adventurers who resolve the situation would earn rewards from all involved.
